diff.argv_shape: rac diff <old> <new> [--json] [--version]. Two required positionals (old, new), each a path to a single file. Parents: version_parent, json_parent. No --sarif, no --template, no directory/stdin support.
diff.exit_codes: 0 on any completed diff (including 'No changes.'). 2 (EXIT_USAGE) for: missing positional (argparse 'the following arguments are required: old' / 'old, new'); a named file that is not is_file() -> hand-written 'rac: file not found: <path>' (checked old first, then new); an unreadable/oversize file that parses to an 'unreadable-artifact' issue -> 'rac: cannot read <path>'; extra positional -> TOP-LEVEL argparse error (prog 'rac', 'unrecognized arguments: <x>') listing every subcommand. Never returns 1.
diff.stdout_kinds: HUMAN (stdout): blocks joined by blank lines; NO ANSI when piped (color gated on sys.stdout.isatty()). Section titles are bold-wrapped only under a tty. First line e.g. 'Added Requirements' then '' then '+ REQ-3 Partial refunds allowed.'; removed use '- '; modified block: '~ REQ-1' / '' / 'Before:' / '<old_text>' / '' / 'After:' / '<new_text>', multiple modifieds separated by an extra blank line. Then 'Added Metrics'/'Removed Metrics'/'Added Risks'/'Removed Risks' blocks (each only if non-empty). Empty diff prints exactly 'No changes.'. JSON (stdout, json.dumps indent=2): keys in fixed order old,new,added_requirements,removed_requirements,modified_requirements,added_metrics,removed_metrics,added_risks,removed_risks. added/removed_requirements items = {id,text,line} (Requirement asdict); modified items = {id,old_text,new_text} (RequirementChange asdict, no line). old/new echo the raw argv path strings. Errors -> stderr.
parity_cases: diff old.md new.md (fixtures with 1 modified REQ-1, +REQ-3, -REQ-2, +1/-1 metric, +1 risk) -> exit 0, human block output
diff old.md new.md --json -> exit 0, byte-exact indent=2 JSON with line numbers from parse
diff old.md old.md -> exit 0, stdout exactly 'No changes.'
diff old.md old.md --json -> exit 0, all eight arrays empty, old==new echo path
diff old.md (missing 2nd positional) -> exit 2, argparse 'usage: rac diff ...' + 'rac diff: error: the following arguments are required: new'
diff nope.md new.md -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: file not found: nope.md' (old checked first)
diff old.md nope.md -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: file not found: nope.md'
diff /some/dir new.md -> exit 2, 'rac: file not found: /some/dir' (is_file() false for dirs)
diff - new.md -> exit 2, 'rac: file not found: -' (no stdin support)
diff old.md new.md extra.md -> exit 2, TOP-LEVEL 'rac: error: unrecognized arguments: extra.md' with full subcommand list
diff old.txt new.txt -> exit 0 (NO extension check, unlike inspect/improve)
diff.landmines: (1) diff has NO git/revisions dependency despite the task hint — services/diff.py is pure AST diff on two file paths; revisions.py belongs to watchkeeper only. (2) diff accepts ANY file extension (uses parse_file directly, no .md guard) — differs from inspect/improve which enforce .md/.markdown. (3) old is _read() before new, so a bad old wins the error. (4) requirements match by id (last-wins on dup id); added/modified iterate NEW order, removed iterates OLD order; metrics/risks are ordered set-difference, de-duped, preserving source order. (5) 'line' in JSON is the parse line number and must match the ported parser exactly. (6) extra positional error is emitted by the TOP parser (prog 'rac' + subcommand list), not 'rac diff'.
diff.env_fs_git: Reads two files via parse_file. No env vars consumed (DECIDED_NO_CACHE irrelevant — no cache path). No filesystem writes. No git.
diff.nondeterminism: none — fully deterministic from file bytes; no timestamps, ids, or ordering seams needed.
diff.interactivity: none (no stdin path; '-' is rejected as file-not-found).
diff.reuse: markdown parser (parse_file, Requirement model), output.rs render helpers, pycompat (json indent=2, ANSI-gating on isatty already handled by other rendered commands). Needs a small Diff struct + render_diff_human/json.
diff.blockers: none — all infra (parser, output) already ported.
diff.harness_gaps: none — read-only, stdout/exit comparison already supported; no stdin needed.

inspect.argv_shape: rac inspect <file|dir|-> [--verbose] [--top-level] [--recursive] [--json] [--version]. One positional. --recursive is a no-op default; --top-level disables recursion (dir only). --verbose is single-file only. Parents: version_parent, json_parent.
inspect.exit_codes: Always 0 on a completed inspection (Unknown is a valid outcome; empty dir is valid). 2 (EXIT_USAGE) for: missing positional (argparse); single-file target not is_file() -> 'rac: file not found: <t>'; a file whose suffix is not .md/.markdown (and not '-') -> 'rac: inspect expects a Markdown file; convert it first with: rac ingest <t>'; unreadable file via _read_markdown_input OSError -> 'rac: cannot read <t>: <err>'.
inspect.stdout_kinds: SINGLE-FILE HUMAN: 'Artifact Type: <Type.title()>' then 'Confidence: <conf:.0%>' ('57%','0%','89%') then '' then 'Present Sections:' with '  ✓ <Section.title()>' green lines (or '  (none)'); then if missing '' 'Missing Sections:' '  ✗ <S.title()>' red lines; then optional 'Decision Metadata:' block ('  Status: Accepted' etc, only non-null of Status/Category/Supersedes); then optional 'Relationships:' block ('  Related Decisions:' then '    - ADR-002'). VERBOSE (single-file, only when --verbose AND NOT --json): shows 'Closest match: <display>' for unknown, 'Required Matches:'/'Recommended Matches:' (✓ or '(none)'), 'Missing:' (✗), and 'Score: <req> + 0.5 × <rec> = <points:g> / <ceiling:g> = <round(fit,2)>' then for unknown '(below the 50% threshold → Unknown)'. SINGLE JSON: result.to_dict() indent=2 -> {type,confidence(2dp float),present_sections(snake),missing_sections(snake)} plus additive status/category/supersedes (only if set) and relationships (snake-section -> [refs], only if present). DIRECTORY HUMAN: 'Files Inspected: <n>' '' then one line per ARTIFACT_SPEC 'Requirements: 0','Decisions: 113',... 'Unknown: 0'. DIRECTORY JSON: {schema_version:'1',directory,recursive,summary:{total_files,counts:{per-type incl unknown},unknown},files:[{path,type,confidence}]}. ✓/✗/×/→/• are UTF-8; no ANSI when piped.
parity_cases: inspect req.md (requirement w/ 3 present, 2 missing) -> exit 0, human, 'Confidence: 57%'
inspect req.md --json -> exit 0, confidence 0.57 float, snake_case section arrays
inspect req.md --verbose -> exit 0, 'Score: 1 + 0.5 × 2 = 2 / 3.5 = 0.57'
inspect decision.md --json (with ## Related Decisions + Status) -> status + relationships.related_decisions array
inspect decision.md (human) -> 'Decision Metadata:' block with 'Status: Accepted'
inspect unknown.md (title+prose only) -> 'Artifact Type: Unknown' 'Confidence: 0%' 'Present Sections:' '  (none)'
inspect unknown.md --verbose -> 'Closest match: Requirement' ... 'Score: 0 + 0.5 × 0 = 0 / 3.5 = 0.0' '(below the 50% threshold → Unknown)'
inspect <dir> -> exit 0, 'Files Inspected: N' + per-type counts in ARTIFACT_SPECS order + 'Unknown:'
inspect <dir> --json -> schema_version '1', counts object, files array in walk-sorted order
inspect <dir> --top-level -> only top-level files (nested subdirs excluded); recursive:false in json
inspect - (stdin, piped req.md) -> exit 0, same as single-file
inspect req.md --verbose --json -> JSON wins (verbose suppressed: 'if args.verbose and not args.json')
inspect nope.md -> exit 2 'rac: file not found: nope.md'
inspect foo.txt -> exit 2 'rac: inspect expects a Markdown file; convert it first with: rac ingest foo.txt'
inspect emptydir -> exit 0, 'Files Inspected: 0' all zero
inspect.landmines: (1) --verbose is ignored when --json is also passed (json branch wins) and ignored entirely for directories (dir path uses _emit before verbose is consulted). (2) Directory detection precedes the .md extension check: 'inspect <dir>' works, but a single non-.md FILE is rejected; '-' means stdin (never a dir/extension check). (3) confidence: human uses Python :.0% (rounds half-to-even-ish per format), JSON uses the pre-rounded 2dp float from classify. Rust already rounds via py_round in classify.rs — reuse it. (4) Section names stored normalized (e.g. 'success metrics'); human .title()s them ('Success Metrics'), JSON snake_cases them ('success_metrics'). (5) 'supersedes' stays a TOP-LEVEL scalar in JSON (v0.4.2/ADR-007 exception), NOT inside relationships. (6) Decision metadata only attached when type=='decision'; relationships extracted for any type with a spec (Unknown has none). (7) canonical_value normalizes Status/Category to the spec's canonical spelling case-insensitively. (8) verbose 'chosen' = the TypeScore matching result.type, or scores[0] for unknown; :g formatting drops trailing zeros (2 not 2.0, 3.5 stays 3.5).
inspect.env_fs_git: Reads a file, stdin, or walks a dir (walk_corpus). No writes, no git, no env beyond the parser. DECIDED_NO_CACHE irrelevant (inspect does not touch the derived cache).
inspect.nondeterminism: none — walk_corpus is sorted-path deterministic (already ported as walk.rs); confidence is deterministically rounded.
inspect.interactivity: stdin via '-' (immediate read of sys.stdin) — harness stdin_file feed covers it.
inspect.reuse: classify.rs (classify, score_artifacts, TypeScore, confidence, present/missing_sections), relationships.rs (extract_relationships_full), spec.rs (spec_for, canonical_value, snake, ARTIFACT_SPECS order, RELATIONSHIP_SECTIONS), walk.rs (walk_corpus), identity, output.rs, pycompat. New code is mostly the InspectionResult/DirectoryInspection assembly + 5 renderers (human, verbose, dir-human, json, dir-json).
inspect.harness_gaps: stdin_file feed exists (used for validate -). No new gap; directory fixtures are read-only.

improve.argv_shape: rac improve <file|-> [--json | --template] [--version]. One positional. --json and --template are a mutually-exclusive group. Parent: version_parent ONLY (json NOT via json_parent; it's a local mutex member). No directory support (unlike inspect).
improve.exit_codes: Always 0 on a completed analysis (advisory; suggestions or not, supported or not, unknown or not). 2 (EXIT_USAGE) for: missing positional (argparse); --json with --template (argparse mutex 'argument --template: not allowed with argument --json'); non-is_file() target -> 'rac: file not found: <t>'; non-.md/.markdown suffix -> 'rac: improve expects a Markdown file; convert it first with: rac ingest <t>'; OSError -> 'rac: cannot read <t>: <err>'.
improve.stdout_kinds:
HUMAN (supported type w/ gaps): 'Artifact Type: <Type>' '' 'Missing Required:' then '  - <Section.title()>' each followed by '      • <question>' guidance bullets (or '  (none)'), '' 'Missing Recommended:' same shape; trailing whitespace rstripped. HUMAN (supported, no gaps): 'Artifact Type: <Type>' '' 'Nothing to improve — all expected sections present.' (em-dash). HUMAN (unknown): exactly 'Unable to generate improvement guidance.
Artifact type could not be determined.'. HUMAN (known-but-unsupported spec): 'Artifact Type: <Type>

Improvement guidance is not currently available for this artifact type.' (currently UNREACHABLE — all 5 specs pass supports_improve). JSON: result.to_dict() indent=2 = {type, missing_required(snake), missing_recommended(snake), guidance:{snake_section:[questions]}} — unknown yields all-empty arrays/obj with type 'unknown'. TEMPLATE (--template): for each missing section (required first) '## <Section.title()>

_TODO_' plus, if guidance, '

' + '<!-- <question> -->' lines, blocks joined by '

', trailing '
'. Unknown/unsupported template reuses the same _UNKNOWN_MESSAGE/_unsupported_message text; no-missing template -> '# Nothing to add — all expected sections present.'
parity_cases: improve req.md (missing Problem[req]+Assumptions[rec]) -> exit 0, human with • guidance bullets
improve req.md --json -> exit 0, {type,missing_required:['problem'],missing_recommended:['assumptions'],guidance:{...}}

improve req.md --template -> exit 0, '## Problem

_TODO_

<!-- ... -->' blocks + trailing newline
improve decision.md (supported, only recommended missing) -> 'Missing Required:' '  (none)' then recommended list
improve roadmap.md (complete) -> exit 0, 'Nothing to improve — all expected sections present.'
improve unknown.md -> exit 0, exactly the 2-line 'Unable to generate improvement guidance.' message
improve unknown.md --json -> exit 0, {type:'unknown',missing_required:[],missing_recommended:[],guidance:{}}
improve unknown.md --template -> exit 0, same 2-line unknown message (not a template)
improve req.md --json --template -> exit 2, argparse mutex '--template: not allowed with argument --json'
improve nope.md -> exit 2 'rac: file not found: nope.md'
improve foo.txt -> exit 2 'rac: improve expects a Markdown file; convert it first with: rac ingest foo.txt'
improve - (stdin) -> exit 0, analyses piped markdown
improve.landmines: (1) All five current specs (requirement/decision/roadmap/prompt/design) return supports_improve==True, so the '_unsupported_message' branch is DEAD in practice — port it for fidelity but no real artifact reaches it; only 'unknown' hits the unknown message. (2) --json is a LOCAL mutex member (parent=version_parent only), so there is NO json_parent inheritance — do not add --sarif etc. (3) Human output ends with .rstrip() (no trailing blank line); template output ends with exactly one trailing '\n'. (4) Em-dash '—' in both 'Nothing to improve' strings and the template 'Nothing to add' string; bullet '•' in human guidance; guidance comments wrapped as '<!-- q -->' in templates. (5) guidance dict only includes sections that HAVE guidance (spec.guidance.get(s) truthy); ordering is missing_required then missing_recommended, schema declaration order within each. (6) Extension check is case-insensitive on the suffix (.MD accepted).
improve.env_fs_git: Reads one file or stdin. No writes, no git, no env. No cache.
improve.reuse: classify.rs (classify.type), classify.rs missing_sections, spec.rs (spec_for, guidance, expected/optional, supports_improve = all expected in guidance), output.rs (already renders guidance maps for schema — snake_map_value), pycompat json. New: ImprovementResult assembly + render_improve_human/json/template + the two shared fallback message constants.
improve.harness_gaps: none — read-only; stdin feed already supported.

rename.argv_shape: rac rename <old> <new> <directory> [--apply] [--top-level] [--json] [--version]. THREE required positionals in order old,new,directory (note directory is LAST). --apply writes; default is dry-run. --top-level disables recursion. Parents: version_parent, json_parent.
rename.exit_codes: 2 (EXIT_USAGE) ONLY for argument/IO misuse: missing positional (argparse); directory arg not is_dir() -> 'rac: not a directory: <dir>'. 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED) for EVERY refused plan (plan.ok False): old-ref-not-found, old-ref-ambiguous, new-ref-collides, new-ref-invalid, old-ref-filename-only — including when --apply is passed on a refusal (apply_rename never runs). 0 for a valid dry-run preview AND a successful --apply.
rename.stdout_kinds: DRY-RUN HUMAN (ok, stdout): 'Rename <old> -> <new>' then '='*len(header) then '' 'Target: <path>  (identity field: <frontmatter_id|id_section|id_field>)' then '<R> inbound reference(s), <I> identity edit across <F> file(s).' '' then per file '  <path>' and per edit two lines '    L<n> ✗ <old_line>' (red) '    L<n> ✓ <new_line>' (green), '' 'Dry run — pass --apply to write these edits.'. DRY-RUN JSON (ok, stdout): plan.to_dict() indent=2 with keys directory,recursive,old_ref,new_ref,ok,reason,target_path,identity_field,files_changed,reference_edits,identity_edits,edits[] (each {path,line,old_line,new_line,kind:'reference'|'identity'}), edits sorted by (path,line). REFUSAL HUMAN goes to STDERR: 'Rename <old> -> <new>' '' '✗ Refused: <reason phrase>.' (phrases from _RENAME_REASONS). REFUSAL JSON goes to STDOUT: full plan.to_dict() with ok:false, reason set, empty edits. APPLY HUMAN (stdout): 'Rename <old> -> <new>' '' '✓ Applied: <R> reference(s) and <I> identity edit across <F> file(s).'. APPLY JSON (stdout): result.to_dict() = {directory,old_ref,new_ref,applied,target_path,files_changed,reference_edits,identity_edits}. ✓/✗ UTF-8; no ANSI piped.
parity_cases: rename RAC-AAAAAAAAAAAA RAC-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ <copy> (frontmatter id target, 1 inbound '- RAC-... (foundational)' ref) dry-run human -> exit 0, diff-hunk preview, files UNCHANGED
same --json -> exit 0, edits sorted (identity file sorts before ref file), kinds identity+reference

same --apply -> exit 0, '✓ Applied: 1 reference(s) and 1 identity edit across 2 file(s).', then compare the WRITTEN file tree byte-for-byte (frontmatter id line + the '- RAC-... (foundational)' line rewritten; sibling '- ADR-100' alias line LEFT untouched)
same --apply --json -> exit 0, RenameResult json (no edits array)
rename RAC-NOSUCHXXXXXX RAC-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ <dir> -> exit 1, stderr '✗ Refused: no artifact resolves to that id.'
...--json -> exit 1, stdout plan json ok:false reason:'old-ref-not-found'
rename adr-200 RAC-QQQQQQQQQQQQ <dir-with-two-adr-200> -> exit 1, 'the id is ambiguous — it resolves to more than one artifact.'
rename RAC-AAAAAAAAAAAA RAC-BBBBBBBBBBBB <dir> (new collides w/ sibling id) -> exit 1, 'the new id already names another artifact.'
rename RAC-AAAAAAAAAAAA 'bad id!' <dir> -> exit 1, 'the new id is not a valid identifier.' (fails _NEW_REF_RE before any walk)
rename adr-100 RAC-QQQQQQQQQQQQ <dir> (filename-only alias) -> exit 1, 'the id is only a filename-derived alias — there is no in-file identity to rewrite, and renaming files is out of scope.'
rename RAC-AAAAAAAAAAAA RAC-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ <a-file-not-dir> -> exit 2 'rac: not a directory: <path>'
rename RAC-AAAAAAAAAAAA (missing new+directory) -> exit 2 argparse 'required: new, directory'
rename RAC-NOSUCHXXXXXX RAC-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ <dir> --apply -> exit 1 (refusal path, nothing written)
rename RAC-AAAAAAAAAAAA RAC-ZZZZZZZZZZZZ <nested-corpus> --top-level -> exit 1 old-ref-not-found (subdir not walked), recursive:false in json
rename.landmines: (1) rename WRITES on --apply — must be sandboxed to a per-engine writable copy; the harness runs both engines against the SAME cwd, so oracle's write would corrupt the rust run's input. (2) Refusal routing is split: human refusal -> STDERR, but JSON refusal -> STDOUT (unlike ok cases which both go stdout); exit is 1 for all refusals, 2 only for not-a-directory/argparse. (3) Positional ORDER is old, new, directory (directory LAST) — easy to mis-wire. (4) _NEW_REF_RE = ^[A-Za-z][\w.-]*$ is checked and new_ref.strip()'d BEFORE the corpus walk, so an invalid new short-circuits (no resolution). (5) Token-specific rewrite: only references whose leading token EQUALS old_ref (case-insensitive, whole-token via next-char class check incl _-.) are rewritten; a sibling line naming a DIFFERENT alias of the same target is left untouched. (6) Identity precedence for the rewrite: frontmatter id (only if metadata.id casefold==old) -> '## ID' section first-value -> spec.id_field section (no spec sets id_field, dead) -> else filename-only refusal. (7) Frontmatter id must match canonical ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}-[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{12}$ to be an identifier at all (Crockford, excludes I,L,O,U and 0/1 in the tail) — a malformed id is NOT in the resolution index. (8) edits sorted by (path,line); reference edits built first then identity appended, then sorted. (9) apply preserves original final-newline shape and verifies old_line matches (raises ValueError on drift). (10) A no-op rename where new casefold==old skips the collision check. (11) casefold-length-safety in _replace_token (slices by len(old_ref) on the folded prefix only).
rename.env_fs_git: Reads dir via walk_corpus + raw file text per artifact. --apply WRITES edited files in place (line replacement, newline-shape preserved). No git. No env beyond parser. No cache.
rename.nondeterminism: none in output — edit set is sorted (path,line); no timestamps/minted ids. The ONLY nondeterminism risk is filesystem mutation ordering, which is irrelevant to bytes.
rename.reuse: identity.rs (artifact_identifiers, id precedence, id_field dead-path already ported), relationships.rs (resolution_index_from_rows / build_resolution_index, RELATIONSHIP_SECTIONS, ValidationRow), walk.rs (walk_corpus sorted), spec.rs (spec_for, optional sections), markdown parser, output.rs, pycompat. New: compute_rename orchestration, raw-line relationship-line scanning (_relationship_reference_lines), _replace_token, frontmatter/id-section/named-section identity edits, apply_rename writer, and 4 renderers (rename human/json, result human/json) + _RENAME_REASONS phrase table.
rename.blockers: none technical — all engine infra ported. The dependency is HARNESS: parity for --apply needs written-file comparison + per-engine writable fixture copies (see harness_gaps).
rename.harness_gaps: MAJOR for --apply: the harness compares stdout/stderr/exit only, runs both engines against one shared cwd, and does NOT (a) stage a fresh writable copy per engine nor (b) compare the resulting file tree. To pin rename --apply we need: a writable staging dir cloned per engine-run under the XDG/tmp sandbox, and a post-run directory byte-comparison. Dry-run and all refusal cases need NEITHER (read-only) and are pinnable today with the existing stdout/stderr/exit comparison. Ambiguous-case fixture needs two artifacts sharing an alias in separate subdirs.

gate.argv_shape: rac gate DIRECTORY [--json | --sarif] [--top-level] [--version]. DIRECTORY is a REQUIRED positional (no default — unlike doctor/coverage which default to '.'). --json and --sarif are a mutually-exclusive group. --top-level flips recursive off. Inherits version_parent (--version).
gate.exit_codes: 0 = ok (no blocking findings). 1 = EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED, on (a) any blocking finding present, or (b) MalformedRepositoryConfig caught in cmd_gate. 2 = EXIT_USAGE, on not-a-directory (_usage_error), missing DIRECTORY positional, or --json+--sarif conflict (argparse). Measured: missing arg exit=2; not-a-dir exit=2; --json --sarif exit=2; malformed config exit=1; broken-relationship corpus exit=1; clean corpus exit=0.
gate.stdout_kinds:
HUMAN (stdout, plain — colours gated on sys.stdout.isatty(), so piped/CI output carries NO ANSI): header block `Corpus Gate
===========

Directory:  <dir>
Blocking:   <n>
Advisory:   <m>`, then optional `
Blocking (<n>)
----...` and `
Advisory (<m>)
----...` groups. Each finding is two lines: `  <icon> <path>` (icon `✗` blocking / `!` advisory) and `      [<source>] <code>: <message>`. Final line: pass `✓ Gate passed — nothing blocking.` / fail `✗ Gate failed — <n> blocking finding(s).`. Empty corpus prints `Blocking:   0 / Advisory:   0` then the pass line. JSON (--json, stdout): json.dumps(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) of {schema_version:'1', directory, recursive, ok, blocking_count, advisory_count, findings:[{source,code,severity,enforcement,path,line,message}]}; note the human `—` em-dash is `—` in JSON message. SARIF (--sarif, stdout): SARIF 2.1.0 doc; `runs[0].tool.driver.version` embeds the setuptools-scm package version e.g. `0.1.dev50+g21c8be403`. ERROR (stderr): not-a-dir `rac: not a directory: <path>`; malformed config `rac: <MalformedRepositoryConfig message>` (see landmines); argparse usage `usage: rac gate [-h] [--version] [--json | --sarif] [--top-level] directory
rac gate: error: <detail>`.
parity_cases: argv=[gate, <fixture-clean-corpus>] env=parity -> exit 0, human header Blocking:0/Advisory:N + `✓ Gate passed — nothing blocking.`
argv=[gate, <fixture>, --json] -> exit 0, byte-compare JSON {ok:true, blocking_count, advisory_count, findings[]}

argv=[gate, <fixture-broken-rel>] where a decision has `## Related Decisions\n- ADR-99999` -> exit 1, human shows Blocking group with `✗ ... [relationships] relationship-target-not-found: related decisions: ADR-99999 — target not found` and `[review] broken-relationship: ...`, verdict `✗ Gate failed — 2 blocking finding(s).`
argv=[gate, <fixture>, --sarif] -> exit 0, SARIF (version field must be pinned/stubbed to match)
argv=[gate, <fixture>, --json, --sarif] -> exit 2, stderr `rac gate: error: argument --sarif: not allowed with argument --json`
argv=[gate] (no positional) -> exit 2, stderr two-line argparse `usage: rac gate ...` + `the following arguments are required: directory`
argv=[gate, /tmp/does-not-exist] -> exit 2, stderr `rac: not a directory: /tmp/does-not-exist`
argv=[gate, <fixture with .decided/config.yaml enforcement: "nope">] -> exit 1, stderr `rac: malformed repository config <abs-path>/.decided/config.yaml: 'enforcement' must be a mapping`
argv=[gate, <fixture with enforcement.blocking: "x">] -> exit 1, stderr `rac: malformed repository config <path>: 'enforcement.blocking' must be a list of finding-code strings`
argv=[gate, <fixture with enforcement.off: [some-code]>] -> exit 0/1, verify the named finding code is dropped from findings (policy suppression)
gate.landmines:
(1) MALFORMED-YAML MESSAGE: an unparseable .decided/config.yaml yields `rac: malformed repository config <path>: invalid YAML: <PyYAML exception>` where the exception is PyYAML's exact MULTILINE text (e.g. `while parsing a flow sequence
  in "<unicode string>", line 2, column 14:
    enforcement: [this is...
             ^`). Byte-reproducing libyaml/PyYAML error prose in Rust is infeasible — the structural messages ('enforcement' must be a mapping / '<key>' must be a list of finding-code strings / 'validation' must be a mapping) ARE deterministic and reproducible; the raw-YAML-parse-error case is not. (2) not-a-dir check runs BEFORE config load, so not-a-dir on a corpus with a malformed config still exits 2, not 1. (3) --sarif version field is git-derived (setuptools-scm) nondeterminism. (4) colour is gated by module-level `_USE_COLOR = sys.stdout.isatty()` — the Rust port must emit plain text when stdout is not a TTY (all captured bytes are plain). (5) YAML 1.1 coerces a bare `off:` key to boolean False; load_enforcement_policy reads both `off` and False keys. (6) findings sort key is (path, line or 0, source, code, message) — stable across the three sources.
gate.env_fs_git: ENV: none. FS reads: walks DIRECTORY for *.md (recursive unless --top-level); reads nearest `.decided/config.yaml` at-or-above DIRECTORY via find_config_file (enforcement policy + validation severity overrides). No writes. GIT: none (gate does not touch git).
gate.nondeterminism: Fully deterministic given corpus + config, EXCEPT the --sarif driver.version string (setuptools-scm/git). Seam needed: pin/stub the package version for --sarif (shared with every other *-sarif command).
gate.reuse: Heavy reuse: rust validate.rs (validate_corpus), relationships.rs (validation_from_corpus + RELATIONSHIP_SEVERITY + SARIF _relationship_result), review.rs (review_from_portfolio, PRIORITY_BROKEN_RELATIONSHIP), portfolio.rs (portfolio_from_corpus), output.rs + existing SARIF/JSON renderers, walk.rs. NEW: an enforcement-policy loader (parse .decided/config.yaml `enforcement:` blocking/advisory/off + `validation` overrides) and a MalformedRepositoryConfig error type mapped to `rac: <msg>`/exit 1.
gate.blockers: none — all three underlying services (validate, relationships, review/portfolio) already exist in rust/rac-engine/src.
gate.harness_gaps: Needs: (a) a `.decided/config.yaml` fixture seam with malformed variants (structural + raw-YAML); (b) a comparison mode that tolerates the raw-YAML PyYAML message (substring / normalized match) since exact bytes are infeasible; (c) version-string pinning for --sarif (already required by other sarif commands).

doctor.argv_shape: rac doctor [DIRECTORY] [--hub-threshold N] [--json] [--top-level] [--version]. DIRECTORY optional positional, default '.'. --hub-threshold int default 20 (DEFAULT_HUB_THRESHOLD). Inherits version_parent, json_parent (--json), scope_parent (--top-level).
doctor.exit_codes: 0 = ok when NO error-severity finding present (orphan/hub/injection/unlinked/suspect warnings all exit 0). 1 = EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED when any error-severity finding present (invalid-artifact, or a relationship-integrity error like relationship-target-not-found). 2 = EXIT_USAGE for not-a-directory. Measured: clean/warnings-only exit=0; broken-relationship exit=1; not-a-dir exit=2.
doctor.stdout_kinds:
HUMAN (stdout): `Repository health: <dir>

`. If no findings: `✓ No issues found.`. Else: `<E> error(s), <W> warning(s)

`, then per finding three lines: `ERROR    <path>` or `WARNING  <path>` (note the two-space vs. label alignment: `ERROR  ` has trailing spaces to width 7, `WARNING` is 7), `  [<code>] <problem>`, `  fix: <fix>`, blank line; then verdict `✓ No errors (warnings are advisory).` (ok) or `✗ Errors present.` (error). JSON (--json): json.dumps(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) of {schema_version:'1', directory, hub_threshold, ok, summary:{errors,warnings}, findings:[{path,code,severity,problem,fix}]}. ERROR (stderr): `rac: not a directory: <path>`. Sample finding lines (live rac/): `WARNING  rac/decisions/adr-002-ai-optional.md` / `  [high-fan-out-hub] high-fan-out hub: 81 resolved relationship edges (threshold 20)`; error case `  [relationship-target-not-found] relationship-target-not-found via 'related_decisions' -> 'ADR-99999'` / `  fix: Run: rac relationships <dir> --validate`.
parity_cases: argv=[doctor, <fixture-clean, single orphan decision, NON-git tmp dir>] -> exit 0, human `0 error(s), 1 warning(s)` + one orphaned-artifact WARNING + `✓ No errors (warnings are advisory).`
argv=[doctor, <fixture>, --json] -> exit 0, byte-compare {schema_version, hub_threshold:20, ok:true, summary, findings[]}
argv=[doctor, <fixture-broken-rel>] -> exit 1, human `1 error(s), 1 warning(s)`, ERROR relationship-target-not-found line + `✗ Errors present.`

argv=[doctor, <fixture-injection>] artifact containing e.g. `ignore all previous instructions` -> exit 0, injection-style-content WARNING with the matched pattern name in the problem, e.g. `instruction-like / injection-style content for review (instruction-override)`
argv=[doctor, <fixture>, --hub-threshold, 0] -> exit 0, every node with >0 edges flagged high-fan-out-hub (threshold shown in problem text)
argv=[doctor, rac/, --top-level] -> exit 0, 0 findings (`✓ No issues found.`) because rac/ has no top-level *.md
argv=[doctor, /tmp/does-not-exist] -> exit 2, stderr `rac: not a directory: /tmp/does-not-exist`

argv=[doctor, <fixture with unlinked body ref>] artifact body mentions ADR-110 with no Related Decisions link -> exit 0, unlinked-reference WARNING `body references ADR-110 but declares no Related Decisions link to it`
argv=[doctor, <GIT-FIXTURE with pinned commit dates, target committed after referrer>] -> suspect-artifact WARNING naming newer target + both commit dates (requires deterministic git history)
doctor.landmines: (1) GIT/RECENCY: `_suspect_artifact_findings` calls `suspect_drift` which shells out to git for commit recency PER relationship edge — on the live rac/ corpus this produced 34 suspect-artifact findings AND made a double `doctor rac/` run exceed a 2-minute timeout. The finding set + the commit-date strings in `problem` depend on git history, so it is NON-DETERMINISTIC across clones/checkouts and byte-parity requires a git fixture with pinned author/commit dates. Outside git it yields nothing and exits 0 cleanly (verified). (2) INJECTION regexes: six deterministic line-scoped (no DOTALL) patterns (instruction-override, role-reassignment, ai-impersonation, chat-role-injection, conceal-from-user, decision-steering) — the matched pattern NAME appears in the problem text, so the Rust regexes must match Python's `re` semantics (IGNORECASE, MULTILINE for chat-role) exactly. (3) orphan count must equal the portfolio's 'never a resolved target' count (a Python test pins this) — reuse the same degree pass. (4) findings sort key: (severity_rank {error:0,warning:1}, path, code, problem). (5) label spacing: `ERROR  ` (7 wide incl. trailing spaces) vs `WARNING`. (6) colour: human output here has NO colour codes (plain) — doctor's renderer builds plain strings, no _red/_green.
doctor.env_fs_git: ENV: none. FS reads: walks DIRECTORY for *.md (recursive unless --top-level); reads artifact bodies for injection + unlinked-reference scan. No writes. GIT: YES — suspect_drift runs git to compute per-artifact commit recency over the resolved relationship graph (via rust gitinfo.rs equivalent). Degrades to empty (no findings, exit 0) outside a git repo.
doctor.nondeterminism: suspect-artifact findings + their commit-date problem strings are git-history-dependent (BLOCKER for byte-parity without a pinned git fixture). Everything else (validation, relationships, hubs, orphans, injection, unlinked) is deterministic. Seam needed: a git fixture with fixed GIT_AUTHOR_DATE/GIT_COMMITTER_DATE, or a way to disable/stub the drift phase for non-git parity runs.
doctor.reuse: Reuse: rust validate.rs (validate_directory/STATUS_INVALID), relationships.rs (validate_relationships, relationships_from_corpus, RELATIONSHIP_SEVERITY, ISSUE_DUPLICATE_IDENTIFIER/CYCLE), gitinfo.rs (recency), walk.rs/corpus, spec.rs (spec_for), output.rs. NEW ports required: links.py (unlinked-reference detection, ~140 LOC), drift.py (suspect_drift + drift_problem over git recency, ~111 LOC), the six injection regexes, and the degree/orphan/hub one-hop pass. No existing rust links/drift module.
doctor.blockers: Git-recency nondeterminism (suspect-artifact) is the parity blocker — needs a deterministic git fixture. No missing INFRA otherwise (gitinfo already ported); links + drift + injection are net-new code, not blocked deps.
doctor.harness_gaps: Needs: (a) home-dir/git-isolated fixture with pinned commit dates so suspect-artifact is reproducible (current harness runs against the real repo where history drifts); (b) tolerance for doctor's slowness (git subprocess per edge) or a cached-recency seam; (c) a non-git fixture case to pin the exit-0 clean path.

coverage.argv_shape: rac coverage [DIRECTORY] [--json] [--version]. DIRECTORY optional positional, default '.'. Inherits version_parent + json_parent ONLY. NO --top-level (always recursive=True), NO --sarif.
coverage.exit_codes: 0 ALWAYS on a valid run — coverage is advisory and NEVER fails (cmd_coverage hardcodes `return EXIT_OK`), even with hundreds of gaps (live rac/ = 105 gaps, exit 0). 2 = EXIT_USAGE only for not-a-directory. Passing an unknown flag like --top-level bubbles to the TOP-LEVEL argparse parser -> exit 2 with the main `usage: rac ...` message (not a coverage-specific usage).
coverage.stdout_kinds: HUMAN (stdout): `Traceability coverage — <dir>\n\n` (note em-dash). If no gaps: `✓ No coverage gaps — every artifact has its expected traceability edge.`. Else, up to three groups in fixed order (unscheduled, unapplied, unscoped), each `<heading>: <count>` then per gap `  <id>  <path>`, blank line between groups; final summary `<total> coverage gap[s] (<u> unscheduled, <a> unapplied, <s> unscoped) — advisory, not a build failure.` (singular 'gap' when total==1). Headings: `Unscheduled requirements (no roadmap schedules them)`, `Unapplied decisions (no requirement or roadmap applies them)`, `Unscoped roadmaps (reference no requirement)`. JSON (--json): json.dumps(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) of {schema_version:'1', directory, gaps:[{path,id,type,gap,missing}], summary:{unscheduled,unapplied,unscoped,total}}. ERROR (stderr): `rac: not a directory: <path>`.
parity_cases: argv=[coverage, <fixture: 1 unscheduled requirement>] -> exit 0, human Unscheduled group + summary `1 coverage gap (1 unscheduled, 0 unapplied, 0 unscoped) — advisory, not a build failure.` (singular)
argv=[coverage, <fixture>, --json] -> exit 0, byte-compare {gaps[], summary:{unscheduled,unapplied,unscoped,total}}
argv=[coverage, <fixture: decision with no incoming requirement/roadmap>] -> exit 0, Unapplied decisions group; missing text `no requirement or roadmap applies this decision`
argv=[coverage, <fixture: roadmap referencing no requirement>] -> exit 0, Unscoped roadmaps group; missing `this roadmap references no requirement`
argv=[coverage, <empty dir>] -> exit 0, `Traceability coverage — <dir>\n\n✓ No coverage gaps — every artifact has its expected traceability edge.`
argv=[coverage, /tmp/does-not-exist] -> exit 2, stderr `rac: not a directory: /tmp/does-not-exist`
argv=[coverage, rac/, --top-level] -> exit 2, TOP-LEVEL `usage: rac [-h] [--version] {validate,...}` + `unrecognized arguments` (argparse bubble, NOT a coverage usage line)
argv=[coverage, <fixture with mixed gaps>] -> verify group order is always unscheduled→unapplied→unscoped and within-group sort is ascending path
coverage.landmines: (1) ALWAYS exits 0 — never conflate with gate/doctor exit semantics; gaps are advisory. (2) The unknown-flag case (--top-level, --sarif) is rejected by the MAIN parser, so the usage/error bytes are the top-level `rac` usage, not `rac coverage ...` — different from gate's own-parser errors. (3) gap classification is edge-typed: unscheduled = requirement with no INCOMING roadmap source-type; unapplied = decision with no incoming {requirement,roadmap}; unscoped = roadmap with no OUTGOING requirement target-type; self-edges (resolved_path == source_path) are skipped. (4) sort: (gap-class order {unscheduled:0,unapplied:1,unscoped:2}, ascending path). (5) em-dash `—` in header/summary is `—`. (6) singular/plural on `gap`/`gaps` by total==1.
coverage.env_fs_git: ENV: none. FS reads: walks DIRECTORY for *.md recursively (walk_corpus), builds index (id/type/path) + relationship graph. No writes. GIT: none.
coverage.nondeterminism: None — fully deterministic offline; no git, no timestamps, no minted ids. No seams needed.
coverage.reuse: Reuse: rust relationships.rs (relationships_from_corpus + resolved_path/source_path), walk.rs/corpus, identity.rs/classify.rs (artifact id + type). NEW: an `index_from_corpus`-equivalent producing (path,id,type) per artifact and the incoming/outgoing type-set pass + three gap rules + renderers. Small, self-contained.
coverage.harness_gaps: Minimal — needs small fixtures exercising each gap class and the clean/empty path. One nuance: the --top-level rejection asserts the TOP-LEVEL argparse usage bytes, so the harness must compare against the main-parser usage string (shared argparse-parity behaviour), not a per-subcommand one.

portfolio.argv_shape: rac portfolio <directory> [--json] [--top-level|--recursive] [--version]. `directory` is a REQUIRED positional (unlike index/export which default to '.'). scope_parent supplies --top-level (recursive=not top_level) and --recursive (affirms default); json_parent supplies --json; version_parent supplies --version.
portfolio.exit_codes: 0 always on a real directory (happy path, empty corpus, invalid artifacts present — portfolio REPORTS health, it never gates). 2 (EXIT_USAGE) for `not a directory: <dir>` (Path.is_dir() false) and for argparse errors (missing positional, bad flag). Measured: not-a-dir -> 2; missing positional -> 2.
portfolio.stdout_kinds: HUMAN on stdout: first line `Repository Summary`, then `==================`, blank, `Directory:  rac/`, `Artifacts:  426`, blank, `By Type`/`-------` then only nonzero types as `  Requirement    87` (type.title() left-just width 14, then count), sections Validation/Completeness/Relationships, then either an Attention block (`  ! RAC-KTQ63DPSMF19` / `      Missing recommended sections: ...` per item; error items use `  ✗ <id>`) or `✓ No attention items.`, then `Health Score`/`  95 / 100`. Empty corpus appends a final blank + `No artifacts yet — create your first with: rac quickstart`. Completeness/coverage lines use `:.0%` (`86%`, `100%`). Color helpers (bold/red/yellow/green) emit NOTHING when stdout is not a TTY (parity pipes -> plain bytes). JSON (--json): `json.dumps(to_dict(), indent=2)`, key order fixed: schema_version, directory, recursive, empty, artifacts{total,by_type{requirement,decision,roadmap,prompt,design,unknown},unknown_paths[]}, validation{valid,invalid}, completeness{recommended_slots,filled,ratio}, relationships{total,valid,broken,orphaned,coverage}, attention[]{path,identifier,severity,code,message}, health{score}, validation_status{artifacts_ok,relationships_ok,ok}. Float repr must match Python round(x,4): `"coverage": 0.8554`, `"ratio": 0.8608`, and `1.0` for empty. stderr on error: `rac: not a directory: <dir>\n`.
portfolio.reuse: rac-engine/src/portfolio.rs ALREADY has PortfolioSummary + portfolio_from_corpus (built for review.rs) computing by_type, valid/invalid, slots, attention (sorted error<warning,path,code), relationships summary+gate, health_score. Only NEW code: render_portfolio_human + render_portfolio_json in output.rs, plus a run_portfolio() dispatch arm + is_dir guard. Reuse output.rs color helpers (bold/red/yellow/green,use_color), pycompat::py_format_percent0 for `:.0%`, pyjson float formatting (already used), walk.rs, relationships.rs.
portfolio.env_fs_git: Reads: walks <directory> for *.md (walk_corpus), reads .decided/config.yaml overrides (load_overrides) if present. No writes. No git. Env: DECIDED_NO_CACHE honoured upstream by cache layer but portfolio uses direct walk (build_portfolio_summary -> walk_corpus, no derived cache). No XDG.
portfolio.nondeterminism: None — pure function of corpus bytes + overrides. by_type insertion order is fixed (ARTIFACT_SPECS then 'unknown'); attention sort is deterministic. No timestamps, no ids minted.
portfolio.landmines: (1) `directory` is REQUIRED here but OPTIONAL (default '.') on the sibling index/export parsers — do not copy the nargs='?' pattern. (2) by_type dict is pre-seeded to all 5 spec types + unknown = 0, then incremented; human render prints only count>0, so a type absent from corpus is omitted but still present (as 0) in JSON. (3) Float parity: ratio/coverage are Python round(x,4) then json.dumps default repr — must match pyjson (0.8608, not 0.86080000...; 1.0 not 1). (4) Human uses type_name.title() -> `Requirement`/`Roadmap`; attention identifier is the canonical artifact id or filename stem. (5) Health score color threshold (>=80 green,>=60 yellow) is byte-invisible under pipe but present under TTY — parity harness pipes so plain. (6) `empty` flag = total_artifacts==0; empty-corpus hint appended only in human.
portfolio.harness_gaps: None new. Existing parity-harness (shared xdg-scratch, piped stdio, DECIDED_NO_CACHE) already covers this read-only command. Needs new parity-cases.json entries pointing at rac/ and small fixtures under rust/fixtures or tests/fixtures.
parity_cases: argv [portfolio, rac/] against live corpus -> exit 0, full human render (biggest surface: all 6 type rows, attention block, health 95/100).
argv [portfolio, rac/, --json] -> exit 0, byte-compare full JSON incl. unknown_paths list ordering and float reprs 0.8554/0.8608.
argv [portfolio, rac/, --top-level] -> exit 0, Artifacts: 0 (rac/ has no top-level *.md), empty-corpus branch with hint + 100/100.
argv [portfolio, <empty-tmp-dir>] and same --json -> exit 0, empty=true, ratio 1.0, coverage 1.0, `✓ No attention items.`, hint line.
argv [portfolio, /tmp/does-not-exist] -> exit 2, stderr `rac: not a directory: /tmp/does-not-exist`.
argv [portfolio] (missing positional) -> exit 2, argparse usage+error on stderr.
a small fixture corpus with 1 invalid artifact + 1 broken relationship -> exercises error-severity attention item (`✗`) and broken-relationship attention message, and health<100.

mcp-stats.argv_shape: rac mcp-stats [--json | --share] [--version]. --json and --share are a mutually-exclusive group; no positional. Guide-only (reads only the Guide telemetry log, not the CLI-usage log).
mcp-stats.exit_codes: 0 always for any log state incl. empty/missing (telemetry off is a valid answer). 2 for the argparse mutex conflict `--json --share` and for unknown flags. Measured: empty->0, populated->0, --share->0, --json --share->2.
mcp-stats.stdout_kinds:
HUMAN: `Guide Telemetry` / `===============` / blank / `Log: <XDG_STATE_HOME>/rac/guide-telemetry.jsonl`. If event_count==0: blank, `No telemetry recorded.`, `Telemetry is off by default; enable it with: rac mcp --telemetry` (+ `

Skipped Unreadable Lines: N` only if skipped>0). Else: `Events: 3` / `Sessions: 2` / `First Event: <ts>` / `Last Event: <ts>` / blank / `Tool Usage` / `==========` / blank / one line per tool `  <tool>: N call(s), N error(s), N truncated, avg N ms` (tools sorted by name), then `

Skipped Unreadable Lines: N` iff skipped>0. JSON (--json): `json.dumps(to_dict(), indent=2)` keys: schema_version, path, event_count, session_count, first_ts, last_ts, skipped_lines, tools[]{tool,calls,errors,truncated,avg_duration_ms}. `first_ts`/`last_ts` are null when empty. --share: prints ONE line, the prefilled GitHub issue URL `https://github.com/itsthelore/rac-core/issues/new?template=guide-usage-report.yml&report=<urlencoded JSON minus the path field>`. stderr: mutex error `rac mcp-stats: error: argument --share: not allowed with argument --json`.
mcp-stats.reuse: No existing Rust port of telemetry summarize/share. Reuse: pyjson (indent=2 + null), output.rs bold() for human headers (byte-invisible under pipe). NEW: telemetry_path() XDG_STATE_HOME helper, read_events() JSONL parser (skip blank, skip non-JSON counting skipped, skip non-dict counting skipped), summarize() (session set size, sorted ts min/max, by_tool sorted, errors=outcome in {error,exception}, truncated==true count, avg_duration_ms=round(mean of int durations)), and a Python-parity urlencode(quote_plus) helper for share_url. output.rs already has a `quote(safe="/")` percent-encoder but NOT quote_plus.
mcp-stats.env_fs_git: Reads XDG_STATE_HOME (fallback $HOME/.local/state) + /rac/guide-telemetry.jsonl. NO writes, no git. No consent check (mcp-stats reads the log regardless of consent).
mcp-stats.nondeterminism: The output `path` embeds XDG_STATE_HOME (absolute). Under the parity harness both engines share one xdg-scratch root, so path is identical A-vs-B (not golden-stable, but parity is A==B). No ids/timestamps minted — all values are read from the seeded log.
mcp-stats.landmines: (1) share_url uses Python `urllib.parse.urlencode({template,report})` == quote_plus per value: space->`+`, and safe set is letters/digits/`_.-~` only (so `:`->`%3A`, `{`->`%7B`, newline->`%0A`, `"`->`%22`, `[`->`%5B`). Byte-exact reproduction of quote_plus is the main risk; captured sample begins `...report=%7B%0A++%22schema_version%22%3A+%221%22%2C%0A++%22event_count%22%3A+3...`. (2) share_url DELETES the `path` key before dumping (report is counts+timestamps only) and dumps with ensure_ascii=False, indent=2. (3) avg_duration_ms only averages values where duration_ms is an int; empty->0; uses round() (banker's rounding parity via pycompat if a .5 case arises). (4) errors counts outcome in {'error','exception'}; truncated counts `truncated is True` exactly. (5) skipped_lines counts BOTH non-JSON lines and JSON-but-not-dict lines; blank lines are NOT counted. (6) tools sorted by tool name (Python sorted()).
mcp-stats.harness_gaps: Need a per-case STATE-FILE SEED mechanism: write a fixture guide-telemetry.jsonl into XDG_STATE_HOME/rac/ before the run for populated cases. Current harness has no `seed_files` field and reuses ONE xdg-scratch across all cases, so a seeded/written log would leak between cases — need per-case isolated+seedable XDG state (or run mcp-stats/usage/telemetry cases in their own harness pass with fresh dirs). Empty case needs no seed and works today. `--share` cases need only stdout compare (already supported).
parity_cases: argv [mcp-stats] with NO log present -> exit 0, `No telemetry recorded.` branch; path line embeds shared xdg-scratch (identical A==B).
argv [mcp-stats, --json] no log -> exit 0, event_count 0, first_ts/last_ts null, tools [].

seed guide-telemetry.jsonl (3 valid events across 2 sessions, 1 error, 1 truncated, + 1 garbage line) then argv [mcp-stats] -> exit 0, Events:3 Sessions:2, two tool lines sorted, `Skipped Unreadable Lines: 1`.
same seed, argv [mcp-stats, --json] -> byte-compare full JSON incl. avg_duration_ms rounding (search_artifacts avg 10 from 12,8).
same seed, argv [mcp-stats, --share] -> exit 0, single URL line; byte-exact quote_plus encoding, path field absent from report.
argv [mcp-stats, --json, --share] -> exit 2, stderr mutex message.

usage.argv_shape: rac usage [--json | --share] [--version]. Mutually-exclusive --json/--share; no positional. Unified read-back over BOTH logs: the CLI-usage log (rac-usage.jsonl) and the Guide log (guide-telemetry.jsonl, via the same summarize as mcp-stats). Guide summary is fetched as a plain dict so no MCP-SDK dependency.
usage.exit_codes: 0 always (empty or missing logs are valid). 2 for `--json --share` mutex and unknown flags. Measured: empty->0, populated->0, --share->0, --json --share->2.
usage.stdout_kinds: HUMAN: `RAC usage` then blank. If cli total==0: `No CLI usage recorded — telemetry is off (enable with \`rac telemetry on\`).`. Else: `CLI commands: <total> calls across <sessions> session(s)`, then per command (sorted by name) `  <command:<16> <calls>` with ` (N error)`/` (N errors)` suffix only when errors>0 (note: `error` singular vs `errors` plural), then if recent nonempty `  recent: 2026-07-10: 2, 2026-07-11: 1`. Then, if guide has tools: blank, `Guide MCP tools:`, per tool `  <tool:<16> <calls>` with ` (N error(s))` suffix when errors>0 (always the `(s)` form here, unlike CLI). JSON (--json): `json.dumps(_combined, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)` = {schema_version, cli:{schema_version,total,sessions,commands[]{command,calls,errors},recent{date:count}}, guide:<TelemetrySummary.to_dict() or {}>}. --share: single prefilled URL line, report is `_combined` (INCLUDES guide.path — usage does NOT strip a path, unlike mcp-stats). stderr: `rac usage: error: argument --share: not allowed with argument --json`.
usage.reuse: Depends on the mcp-stats port (reuses guide summarize()/TelemetrySummary.to_dict()). NEW: usage_path() (XDG_STATE_HOME/rac/rac-usage.jsonl), read_usage() (JSONL, dicts only, tolerant), summarize_usage() (sessions set; by_command sorted; CommandUsage.errors=outcome in {error,exception}; recent = Counter of ts[:10] then sorted().items()[-7:] as dict), render_human/render_json/share_url. Shares the quote_plus urlencode helper with mcp-stats. Reuse pyjson (indent=2, ensure_ascii=False).
usage.env_fs_git: Reads XDG_STATE_HOME/rac/rac-usage.jsonl AND XDG_STATE_HOME/rac/guide-telemetry.jsonl. No writes, no git, no consent gate on read.
usage.nondeterminism: guide.path embeds XDG_STATE_HOME (identical A==B under shared xdg-scratch). No ids/timestamps minted; recent buckets derive from event ts[:10].
usage.landmines: (1) Two DIFFERENT pluralizations: CLI errors -> `error`/`errors` (via `'s' if errors!=1`); guide errors -> always `error(s)`. Both only shown when >0. (2) Command/tool columns are `{x:<16}` left-justified width 16 (code-point padding, but ids are ASCII). (3) `recent` = last min(7,N) distinct UTC dates: `sorted(day_counts.items())[-days:]` then dict() — insertion order = ascending date. Only rendered in human if nonempty. (4) share_url report is the FULL _combined dict INCLUDING guide.path (mcp-stats strips path; usage does not) — do not copy the strip. (5) Empty guide log still yields a full guide dict (with null first/last_ts) inside JSON, but guide `{}` only when guide arg falsy — cmd always passes guide_summarize().to_dict() so it's the full dict, never {}. (6) ts[:10] bucketing requires len(ts)>=10; recent Counter ignores shorter/malformed ts. (7) sessions counts distinct `session` string values across CLI events only (guide sessions separate).
usage.harness_gaps: Same STATE-FILE SEED gap as mcp-stats, doubled: populated cases must seed BOTH rac-usage.jsonl and guide-telemetry.jsonl into the shared/per-case XDG_STATE_HOME/rac/. Empty case works today. Needs a `seed_files` (dest-relative-to-XDG) harness field and per-case XDG isolation so seeded files don't leak across cases.
parity_cases: argv [usage] with no logs -> exit 0, `No CLI usage recorded...` branch.
argv [usage, --json] no logs -> exit 0, cli.total 0 / commands [] / recent {}, guide full dict with null ts.

seed rac-usage.jsonl (3 events/2 sessions/1 error over commands find+validate + 1 bad line) and guide log (1 event) -> argv [usage] -> exit 0, `find` no-suffix + `validate  (1 error)` singular, `recent:` line, `Guide MCP tools:` section.
same seed, argv [usage, --json] -> byte-compare _combined (commands sorted find<validate, recent two dates).
same seed, argv [usage, --share] -> exit 0, single URL; report includes guide.path (contrast mcp-stats).
argv [usage, --json, --share] -> exit 2 mutex.

telemetry.argv_shape: rac telemetry [action] [--enterprise] [--unlock] [--version]. action is optional positional choices {on,off,status}, default `status`. --enterprise/--unlock are store_true, only meaningful with `off` (ADR-086). This command WRITES consent state.
telemetry.exit_codes: 0 for successful on/off/status incl. enterprise lock/unlock. 2 (EXIT_USAGE) for: `--enterprise`/`--unlock` with action != off; `--unlock` without `--enterprise`; `on` while enterprise-locked (refused); argparse invalid action choice; unrecognized extra args. Measured: on->0, off->0, status->0, off --enterprise->0, off --enterprise --unlock->0, on(while locked)->2, on --enterprise->2, status --enterprise->2, off --unlock->2, bad action->2.
telemetry.stdout_kinds: All messages go to STDOUT except the four usage-error strings (stderr). `on`: `Sharing on. Install id: <32-hex>` then `One anonymous daily ping: install id, rac version, active-repo count. Never paths, queries, or content (ADR-041).` (+ `Note: this build has no PostHog key configured; nothing will be sent.` ONLY if POSTHOG_API_KEY empty — reference build has a key, so this line does NOT appear). `off`: `Sharing off. Nothing will be sent.`. `off --enterprise`: `Sharing off and enterprise-locked. The daily ping is forced off and cannot be re-enabled until unlocked with 'rac telemetry off --enterprise --unlock' (ADR-086).`. `off --enterprise --unlock`: `Enterprise lock removed. Sharing stays off; re-enable with 'rac telemetry on' (ADR-086).`. `status`: 4 fixed lines `Sharing: <off|on|locked (enterprise)>` / `Install id: <hex or (none)>` / `Consented at: <ts or (never)>` / `Consent file: <XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/rac/telemetry.json`, then a 5th line iff locked (`Enterprise lock: on — the daily ping is forced off. Remove with 'rac telemetry off --enterprise --unlock' (ADR-086).`) OR iff sharing (`Shared daily: install id, rac version, active-repo count. Never paths, queries, or content (ADR-041).`), then `Endpoint key: not configured — nothing is sent.` ONLY if POSTHOG_API_KEY empty (absent in reference). STDERR usage errors: `rac: --enterprise/--unlock are only valid with 'rac telemetry off'`; `rac: --unlock requires --enterprise (use 'rac telemetry off --enterprise --unlock')`; `rac: cannot opt in while the enterprise telemetry lock is set; remove it with 'rac telemetry off --enterprise --unlock' first (ADR-086).`.
telemetry.reuse: No existing Rust consent module. NEW port of src/asdecided/consent.py: consent_path() (XDG_CONFIG_HOME fallback $HOME/.config), load_consent (tolerant: bad/missing/non-dict -> default no-consent), save_consent (json.dumps(asdict,indent=2)+'\n', field order share_usage,install_id,salt,consented_at,enterprise_locked), opt_in/opt_out/enterprise_lock/enterprise_unlock/consent_status, POSTHOG_API_KEY constant (must be NON-EMPTY to match endpoint_configured=True / suppress the not-configured lines). Reuse pyjson for indent=2 file write. Random id minting (secrets.token_hex(16)) + UTC timestamp need a seam.
telemetry.env_fs_git: Reads+WRITES XDG_CONFIG_HOME/rac/telemetry.json (mkdir -p parent). No git. `on` mints install_id/salt via secrets.token_hex(16) and consented_at via datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(timespec='seconds') with +00:00->Z. off/lock/unlock preserve existing ids (kept for retention continuity). No XDG_STATE. No network (POSTHOG_* are inert strings; only ping module sends, not this command).
telemetry.interactivity: None (does not prompt; the init prompt is a separate command).
telemetry.nondeterminism: install_id (32 hex), salt (32 hex), consented_at (current UTC to seconds) are minted on `on` -> both the STDOUT `Install id:` line and the WRITTEN telemetry.json vary run-to-run. Needs id+time seams (e.g. DECIDED_RS_INSTALL_ID / DECIDED_RS_SALT / DECIDED_RS_NOW env, mirroring the existing DECIDED_RS_VERSION seam) for A-vs-B parity. off/lock/unlock over a pre-seeded file are fully deterministic (ids preserved verbatim).
telemetry.landmines: (1) Flag validation ORDER in cmd_telemetry: first reject enterprise/unlock with action!=off (exit 2), THEN reject unlock-without-enterprise (exit 2), THEN handle actions; `on` refusal-while-locked is a THIRD exit-2 path with its own message. Reproduce exactly. (2) status `Sharing:` tri-state precedence: enterprise_locked -> `locked (enterprise)` wins over sharing. (3) The 5th status line is mutually exclusive: locked-note XOR sharing-note (locked checked first); never both. (4) consented_at format: isoformat(timespec='seconds') then .replace('+00:00','Z') -> `2026-07-12T18:42:39Z`. (5) Written file: json.dumps(asdict, indent=2) + trailing '\n', exact key order; opt_out/lock/unlock reload existing then rewrite (install_id/salt/consented_at carried through, empty strings when never opted in). (6) POSTHOG_API_KEY must be embedded non-empty in Rust or the two `not configured`/`no PostHog key` lines wrongly appear — reference build's key is set, so those lines are ABSENT in all captured runs. (7) load_consent coerces types (bool/str) and tolerates non-dict JSON -> default. (8) `on` exit code when refused-because-locked is 2, not 1.
telemetry.harness_gaps: Biggest gaps of the four: (a) WRITTEN-FILE capture+compare — harness currently compares only stdout+exit+stderr_len; telemetry.json contents must be diffed A-vs-B (with id/time seam applied). (b) id/timestamp SEAM for `on` (new DECIDED_RS_* env, like DECIDED_RS_VERSION) so minted install_id/salt/consented_at are injected and identical A==B. (c) per-case ISOLATED+SEEDABLE XDG_CONFIG state — the shared xdg-scratch would let one case's write pollute the next; off/lock/unlock cases must be seeded with a specific telemetry.json first. (d) status/off `Consent file:` line embeds the XDG_CONFIG path (identical A==B under shared root).
telemetry.blockers: none for logic; the seam + written-file-compare + per-case config isolation are harness features that must be built before `on`/`off` write-parity can be refereed. status-only and error-path cases can be refereed with the current harness today (they don't write, or write is irrelevant to stdout).
parity_cases: argv [telemetry] and [telemetry, status] with no consent file -> exit 0, 4-line status, Sharing: off, Install id: (none), Consented at: (never).

argv [telemetry, on] with id/time seam pinned -> exit 0, `Sharing on. Install id: <seam>` + ADR-041 line; ALSO diff written telemetry.json (share_usage true, seamed ids, consented_at seam, enterprise_locked false).
seed a share_usage:true file, argv [telemetry, status] -> exit 0, Sharing: on + `Shared daily:` note.
seed share_usage:true file, argv [telemetry, off] -> exit 0, `Sharing off. Nothing will be sent.`; written file share_usage false, ids preserved.
argv [telemetry, off, --enterprise] no file -> exit 0, lock message; file enterprise_locked true, ids empty strings.
seed enterprise_locked:true, argv [telemetry, status] -> Sharing: locked (enterprise) + lock note (5th line).
seed enterprise_locked:true, argv [telemetry, on] -> exit 2, stderr refusal message; file UNCHANGED.
seed enterprise_locked:true, argv [telemetry, off, --enterprise, --unlock] -> exit 0, unlock message; file enterprise_locked false.
argv [telemetry, on, --enterprise] -> exit 2 `--enterprise/--unlock are only valid with 'rac telemetry off'`.
argv [telemetry, off, --unlock] -> exit 2 `--unlock requires --enterprise ...`.
argv [telemetry, bogus] -> exit 2 argparse invalid-choice on stderr.

new.argv_shape: rac new <type> <output_path> [--json] [--version]  — two required positionals (type, output_path); no directory arg (repo config is discovered by walking up from output_path's parent). --json/--version come from shared parents.
new.exit_codes: 0 ok. 2 usage: TemplateNotFound (bad type), OutputPathExists, OutputDirectoryMissing (parent not a dir), MissingRepositoryConfig (no .decided/config.yaml at/above parent), and all argparse errors (missing output_path, extra args). 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED): operational errors TemplateResourceMissing / MalformedRepositoryConfig / IdGenerationExhausted, AND uncaught traceback exit when the id-collision index walk hits hostile markdown (measured exit 1).
new.stdout_kinds: HUMAN stdout (3 lines + blank): 'Created decision artifact: r1/rac/decisions/foo.md' / 'ID: RAC-KXBT74C2FPMM' / '' / 'Edit the TODO placeholders, then check it with: rac validate r1/rac/decisions/foo.md'. JSON stdout (indent=2, NO bytes_written field): {"schema_version": "1", "created": true, "type": "requirement", "path": "r1/rac/requirements/bar.md", "id": "RAC-KXBT74KQC2ER"}. ALL error paths -> stderr plain 'rac: <msg>' regardless of --json, e.g. 'rac: unsupported artifact type: bogus (supported: requirement, decision, roadmap, prompt, design)', 'rac: r1/rac/decisions/foo.md already exists; rac new never overwrites', 'rac: directory does not exist: r1/rac/decisions', 'rac: no repository identity found at or above /tmp/.../noinit; run `rac init` to establish a repository key first'. argparse missing-arg -> stderr 2 lines: 'usage: rac new [-h] [--version] [--json] type output_path' / 'rac new: error: the following arguments are required: output_path'.
parity_cases: happy human: init RAC in DIR; mkdir DIR/rac/decisions; `rac new decision DIR/rac/decisions/foo.md` -> exit 0, stdout 4 lines, id MASKED; written file DIR/rac/decisions/foo.md compared byte-for-byte with id masked (frontmatter + verbatim template body).
happy json: `rac new requirement DIR/rac/requirements/bar.md --json` -> exit 0, JSON body with id masked.
bad type: `rac new bogus DIR/x.md` -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: unsupported artifact type: bogus (supported: requirement, decision, roadmap, prompt, design)'.
output exists: pre-create foo.md then `rac new decision DIR/.../foo.md` -> exit 2, stderr '... already exists; rac new never overwrites'.
parent missing: `rac new decision DIR/nope/x.md` -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: directory does not exist: DIR/nope'.
no repo config: `rac new decision /tmp/uninit/x.md` (dir exists, no .decided) -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: no repository identity found at or above /tmp/uninit; run `rac init` ...'.
missing arg: `rac new decision` -> exit 2, argparse usage+error to stderr.

HOSTILE-MARKDOWN DIVERGENCE (exclude from parity, list in gap): init a repo, drop rust/fuzz/pinned/oracle-crashes/unhashable-key/repro.md into DIR/rac/decisions/case.md, then `rac new decision DIR/rac/decisions/new.md` -> oracle exits 1 with uncaught 'TypeError: unhashable type: list' from frontmatter.py _no_duplicates during the id-collision index walk; Rust must walk past (report internal-oracle-divergence marker) and succeed — parity impossible by design, same class already pinned in oracle-crashes/README.md.
new.landmines: (1) JSON to_dict OMITS bytes_written even though CreatedArtifact carries it — do not emit it. (2) id is nondeterministic (see nondeterminism) and also lands in the WRITTEN frontmatter, so both stdout AND the written file need id-masking to compare. (3) id-collision check walks the WHOLE corpus (build_repository_index) — a hostile-markdown file anywhere under the repo crashes the oracle (exit 1, traceback); Rust must survive it. (4) path is echoed verbatim (no normalization): argv 'r1/rac/decisions/foo.md' appears literally in stdout and JSON. (5) template body must be byte-identical to the packaged .md (verbatim, no trailing-newline munging).
new.nondeterminism: id seam REQUIRED. id = '<KEY>-' + 8-char Crockford-base32 of (int(time.time()*1000) & (2^40-1)) + 4-char base32 of secrets.randbits(20). Alphabet '0123456789ABCDEFGHJKMNPQRSTVWXYZ'. The Python oracle has NO env override for the id (id_generator is injectable only in tests) — so the oracle id CANNOT be pinned from outside. Parity approach: (a) give Rust its own env seam mirroring output.rs's DECIDED_RS_VERSION, e.g. DECIDED_RS_ID, for Rust-side determinism; (b) add a harness id-mask normalization (regex 'RAC-[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{12}' or per-key '<KEY>-[0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{12}') applied to BOTH sides' stdout AND to written-file comparison, since the oracle id is unpinnable.
new.env_fs_git: ENV: none consumed by the handler except the shared cache/telemetry env (DECIDED_NO_CACHE etc) which is irrelevant here; NO git. FS reads: nearest .decided/config.yaml walking up from output_path.parent; full recursive corpus walk (from repo root = config dir's grandparent) to build the id-collision index. FS writes: exactly one file = output_path (utf-8, frontmatter+body).
new.blockers: none — uses the uncached walk (no derived-index/cache, no markitdown, no TUI). Only prerequisite is porting config-file upward discovery and embedding the 5 template bodies.
new.harness_gaps: (1) WRITTEN-FILE COMPARISON: harness compares only stdout/stderr/exit; `new` writes a file that must be byte-compared — needs a per-case filesystem-snapshot-and-diff feature, with each engine running against its OWN writable corpus copy (they cannot share a dir since both write). (2) ID-MASK normalization applied to stdout AND written files (harness already has 'named normalizations applied to both sides' stdout' — extend to file bytes). (3) The hostile-markdown case needs to be registered as an excluded oracle-crash divergence, not a parity case.

init.argv_shape: rac init [directory='.'] [--key KEY(default RAC)] [--ticketing PROVIDER] [--profile NAME] [--json] [--version]. directory is optional positional (nargs='?', default '.'). --ticketing choices: jira, github, linear, azure-devops, servicenow, none. --profile choices: default, enterprise.
init.exit_codes: 0 (created OR idempotent re-init with same key). 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED): RepositoryKeyConflict, MalformedRepositoryConfig. 2 (EXIT_USAGE): InvalidRepositoryKey (via _usage_error), not-a-directory (via _usage_error), and argparse invalid-choice for --ticketing/--profile. NOTE: InvalidTicketingProvider/InvalidProfile exception branches in cmd_init are UNREACHABLE from the CLI because argparse choices reject bad values first (still reachable via the service API).
init.stdout_kinds: HUMAN created (2+ lines): 'Initialized repository key RAC' / 'Config: r2/.decided/config.yaml' then optional 'Profile: enterprise' and one 'Wrote: <path>' per file_written (e.g. 'Wrote: r7/.mcp.json' / 'Wrote: r7/.cursor/mcp.json'). HUMAN idempotent: 'Already initialized: repository key RAC' / 'Config: r1/.decided/config.yaml'. JSON (indent=2): {"schema_version": "1", "repository_key": "RAC", "config_path": "r6/.decided/config.yaml", "created": true, "profile": "default", "files_written": ["r6/.mcp.json", "r6/.cursor/mcp.json"]} (profile null and files_written [] when absent). ERRORS to stderr: conflict (exit 1) 'rac: repository already initialized with key 'RAC' (r1/.decided/config.yaml); refusing to change it to 'OTHER' — established ID namespaces are never silently rewritten'; bad key (exit 2) 'rac: invalid repository key: 'bad' (expected 2-10 uppercase alphanumeric characters starting with a letter, e.g. RAC)'; not-a-dir (exit 2) 'rac: not a directory: /path'. argparse bad choice (exit 2) 2-line usage block: 'usage: rac init [-h] [--version] [--json] [--key KEY] [--ticketing PROVIDER]' / '                [--profile NAME]' / '                [directory]' / "rac init: error: argument --profile: invalid choice: 'bogus' (choose from 'default', 'enterprise')".
init.reuse: output.rs (add render_init_human/json), pyjson.rs (indent=2 dict serialize), spec.rs not needed. New: config.yaml read/parse (yaml.safe_load subset — repository_key str + KEY_RE regex ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$), profiles table (2 profiles, embedded MCP_JSON + _ENTERPRISE_CONFIG stanza as string constants), write_mcp_configs (write .mcp.json and .cursor/mcp.json, never overwrite).
parity_cases: created human: mkdir DIR; `rac init DIR` -> exit 0, stdout 2 lines; compare written DIR/.decided/config.yaml == 'repository_key: RAC\n'.
created json: `rac init DIR --json` -> exit 0, JSON with created true, profile null, files_written [].
idempotent: re-run `rac init DIR` -> exit 0, stdout 'Already initialized: ...'; config unchanged.
key conflict: `rac init DIR --key OTHER` on an RAC-initialized DIR -> exit 1, stderr conflict message.
invalid key: `rac init DIR --key bad` -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: invalid repository key: ...'.
profile default json: `rac init DIR --profile default --json` -> exit 0, files_written [DIR/.mcp.json, DIR/.cursor/mcp.json]; compare both written files == MCP_JSON constant byte-for-byte.

profile enterprise + ticketing: `rac init DIR --profile enterprise --ticketing jira` -> exit 0, human with Profile+2 Wrote lines; compare DIR/.decided/config.yaml == 'repository_key: RAC
ticketing:
  provider: jira
' + enterprise enforcement stanza.
argparse bad choice: `rac init DIR --profile bogus` -> exit 2, 4-line usage+error; same for `--ticketing bogus`.
not a directory: `rac init /nonexistent` -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: not a directory: /nonexistent'.
init.landmines:
(1) directory is echoed verbatim into config_path as '<dir>/.decided/config.yaml' (string join, NOT resolved/absolutized) — 'r2/.decided/config.yaml' with the literal relative arg; default '.' yields '.decided/config.yaml' (the './' is stripped by Path join). (2) files_written paths are also '<dir>/.mcp.json' literal joins. (3) config.yaml body is hand-built string concatenation (NOT a yaml dump): 'repository_key: KEY
' then optional 'ticketing:
  provider: X
' then the profile config_stanza verbatim (the enterprise stanza is a fixed multi-line block with a leading comment) — reproduce byte-for-byte. (4) MCP_JSON and .cursor/mcp.json are IDENTICAL content. (5) never-overwrite: write_mcp_configs skips an existing target and omits it from files_written. (6) idempotent path re-reads and validates the existing config (can raise MalformedRepositoryConfig exit 1). (7) profile/ticketing/stanza only apply on FRESH init; an already-initialized dir ignores them entirely (returns created=false, no files_written).
init.nondeterminism: none — fully deterministic (no id, no timestamp, no git).
init.env_fs_git: ENV: XDG_CONFIG_HOME (only via the post-emit consent prompt gate — see interactivity; the init logic itself reads no env). NO git. FS reads: DIR/.decided/config.yaml if present (idempotent/conflict check). FS writes: DIR/.decided/config.yaml (mkdir -p .decided), and with a profile DIR/.mcp.json + DIR/.cursor/mcp.json (mkdir -p .cursor).
init.interactivity: SOLE interactive prompt (shared with quickstart). After a successful NON-json init, _maybe_ask_usage_sharing() runs: only if sys.stdin.isatty() AND sys.stdout.isatty() AND consent not already recorded. Prompt bytes (captured via PTY): '\nShare anonymous usage to help shape Lore? [y/N] '. Answer y/yes -> opt_in + prints 'Sharing on — one anonymous daily ping. 'rac telemetry status' shows exactly what; 'rac telemetry off' stops it.'; anything else / EOF / empty -> decline (silent). Persisted to $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/rac/telemetry.json so asked at most once per machine. Under the parity harness (piped stdio) isatty is FALSE on both sides so the prompt NEVER fires — not stdout-parity-testable; the port must still implement the exact isatty-both gate and byte-identical prompt/response for real-TTY behavior.
init.blockers: none — deterministic, no derived-index/markitdown/TUI. The consent prompt writes telemetry state but is unreachable under the harness.
init.harness_gaps: (1) WRITTEN-FILE COMPARISON (config.yaml, .mcp.json, .cursor/mcp.json) — not covered by the stdout/stderr/exit-only harness; needs filesystem snapshot+diff with each engine on its own writable copy. (2) HOME/XDG isolation ALREADY EXISTS in parity-harness (XDG_CONFIG_HOME/STATE/CACHE point at scratch, only PATH+HOME inherited) so no consent file is recorded and the prompt is correctly suppressed via piped stdio — no gap here. (3) To parity-test the prompt itself would need a PTY-driven harness mode with stdin feed — currently absent (harness pipes stdin only); recommend leaving the prompt out of the byte-parity suite and covering it with a Rust unit test.

quickstart.argv_shape: rac quickstart [directory='.'] [--key KEY(default RAC)] [--type TYPE(default requirement)] [--json] [--version]. --type has NO choices constraint (free string, validated by load_template) — unlike --profile/--ticketing on init.
quickstart.exit_codes: 0 ok. 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED): CorpusNotEmpty, RepositoryKeyConflict, OutputPathExists, MalformedRepositoryConfig, TemplateResourceMissing, IdGenerationExhausted, AND uncaught traceback exit on hostile markdown during the empty-check walk (measured exit 1). 2 (EXIT_USAGE): TemplateNotFound (bad --type), InvalidRepositoryKey (bad --key), OutputDirectoryMissing, not-a-directory.
quickstart.stdout_kinds: HUMAN (4 lines + blank): 'Initialized repository key RAC' (verb 'Using' when init was idempotent) / 'Created requirement artifact: q1/rac/requirements/first-requirement.md' / 'ID: RAC-KXBT7GQ05SQV' / '' / 'Next: edit the TODO placeholders, then run: rac validate q1/rac/requirements/first-requirement.md'. JSON (indent=2, nested artifact): {"schema_version": "1", "repository_key": "PROJ", "config_path": "q2/.decided/config.yaml", "created": true, "artifact": {"type": "decision", "path": "q2/rac/decisions/first-decision.md", "id": "PROJ-KXBT7GZ603CM"}}. ERRORS to stderr: corpus not empty (exit 1) 'rac: corpus already has artifacts (e.g. q1/rac/requirements/first-requirement.md); rac quickstart only scaffolds an empty corpus — use `rac new` to add more'; bad type (exit 2) 'rac: unsupported artifact type: bogus (supported: requirement, decision, roadmap, prompt, design)'; bad key (exit 2) 'rac: invalid repository key: 'bad' ...'; not-a-dir (exit 2) 'rac: not a directory: /path'.
quickstart.reuse: Composes the ported init + new: init_repository (config write) + create_artifact (frontmatter+body+id) + build_repository_index (walk/classify/parse for the empty-check). output.rs (add render_quickstart_human/json). Once `new` and `init` are ported this is thin glue: validate type, empty-check walk, init, mkdir rac/<type>s/, create first-<type>.md.
parity_cases: happy human empty corpus: mkdir DIR; `rac quickstart DIR` -> exit 0, stdout 4 lines (id masked); compare written DIR/.decided/config.yaml == 'repository_key: RAC\n' AND DIR/rac/requirements/first-requirement.md (frontmatter+verbatim requirement template, id masked).
happy json custom key/type: `rac quickstart DIR --key PROJ --type decision --json` -> exit 0, nested JSON, file at DIR/rac/decisions/first-decision.md.
corpus not empty: run quickstart once, re-run `rac quickstart DIR` -> exit 1, stderr 'rac: corpus already has artifacts (e.g. DIR/rac/requirements/first-requirement.md); ...'.
bad type: `rac quickstart DIR --type bogus` -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: unsupported artifact type: bogus (supported: ...)' — validated BEFORE any write.
bad key: `rac quickstart DIR --key bad` -> exit 2, stderr invalid-key (NOTE: type is validated first, then empty-check, then key inside init).
not a directory: `rac quickstart /nonexistent` -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: not a directory: /nonexistent'.

HOSTILE-MARKDOWN DIVERGENCE (exclude from parity): drop unhashable-key/repro.md into DIR/rac/decisions/case.md then `rac quickstart DIR` -> oracle exits 1 with 'TypeError: unhashable type: list' during the empty-check build_repository_index walk; Rust walks past. Note a semantic subtlety for Rust: after walking past, the hostile file classifies as some type -> if type != 'unknown' Rust would raise CorpusNotEmpty(exit 1) rather than scaffolding; still a divergence from the oracle's crash, so excluded.
quickstart.landmines: (1) ORDER of checks (all before any write): load_template(type) FIRST (TemplateNotFound exit 2) -> build_repository_index empty-check (CorpusNotEmpty exit 1) -> init_repository (InvalidRepositoryKey exit 2 / RepositoryKeyConflict exit 1) -> create_artifact. Get the order right so the right error wins. (2) empty-check uses `e.type != 'unknown'` — a corpus with only unknown/unrecognized files is still 'empty' and gets scaffolded. (3) starter path is DIR/rac/<type>s/first-<type>.md — family is just type+'s' (requirement->requirements, decision->decisions). (4) id nondeterminism (same as `new`) lands in stdout, JSON, and written frontmatter -> mask everywhere. (5) verb switches 'Initialized'/'Using' on init created flag (idempotent init when key pre-established but corpus still empty). (6) config_path and artifact path echo the literal directory arg (no resolve). (7) hostile markdown in the empty-check walk crashes the oracle (exit 1 traceback) — Rust must survive.
quickstart.nondeterminism: id seam REQUIRED — same as `new` (idgen: KEY + 8-char ms-timestamp base32 + 4-char randbits base32; oracle has no env override). Use the same harness id-mask normalization on stdout/JSON/written-file, and the same Rust-side DECIDED_RS_ID env seam for Rust determinism.
quickstart.env_fs_git: ENV: XDG_CONFIG_HOME only via the shared consent prompt (same as init). NO git. FS reads: recursive corpus walk of DIR (empty-check via build_repository_index). FS writes: DIR/.decided/config.yaml, mkdir -p DIR/rac/<family>/, and DIR/rac/<family>/first-<type>.md.
quickstart.interactivity: Same SOLE prompt as init: after a successful NON-json quickstart, _maybe_ask_usage_sharing() with the identical isatty-both + consent-not-recorded gate and identical bytes '\nShare anonymous usage to help shape Lore? [y/N] ' (+ 'Sharing on — ...' on y). Never fires under the piped-stdio harness.
quickstart.blockers: none — uncached walk only; no derived-index/markitdown/TUI. Depends on `new` and `init` being ported first (composition).
quickstart.harness_gaps: (1) WRITTEN-FILE COMPARISON of the two written files (config.yaml + starter artifact) with id-masking, each engine on its own writable copy. (2) ID-MASK normalization on stdout/JSON/files. (3) Hostile-markdown case registered as excluded oracle-crash divergence. (4) Consent prompt not parity-testable under piped harness (same as init) — cover with a Rust unit test.

skill.argv_shape: rac skill <action> [name] [--dir DIR] [--json] [--version]. action is a required positional with choices {install,list}; name is optional positional (default None); --dir default ".". `list` takes no name (extra name -> usage error). `install` with no name installs ALL bundled skills all-or-nothing; with a name installs exactly that one. Bundled skills, registry order: rac-artifacts, rac-review, rac-ingest, rac-import, rac-capture.
skill.exit_codes: 0 success (list, or install). 2 usage error via _usage_error('rac: <msg>' to stderr): `list` given a name -> 'rac: skill list takes no skill name'; target --dir not a directory -> 'rac: not a directory: <dir>'; unknown skill name (dir exists) -> 'rac: unknown skill: bogus (available: rac-artifacts, rac-review, rac-ingest, rac-import, rac-capture)'. 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED) via 'rac: <msg>' to stderr when a target file already exists (SkillFileExists) or a packaged resource is missing (SkillResourceMissing).
