NOTE: the --dir not-a-directory check runs BEFORE the unknown-name check, so `skill install bogus --dir /nonexistent` -> exit 2 'not a directory', not 'unknown skill'.
skill.stdout_kinds: list human (stdout): first line bold-wrapped 'Bundled agent skills:' then blank then per-skill '- <name padded to max width>  <description>' e.g. '- rac-artifacts  Author and maintain Lore (RAC) Markdown artifacts with the rac CLI.' (name field left-justified to width of longest name = len('rac-artifacts')=13, two spaces then description). list --json (stdout, json.dumps indent=2): {"schema_version":"1","skills":[{"skill":..,"description":..},...]}. install human (stdout): one line per installed skill 'Installed <name> skill: <abspath>' then blank then 'Claude Code discovers skills automatically from .claude/skills/ in the project.'. install --json: {"schema_version":"1","installed":true,"skills":[{"skill":..,"path":<abspath>},...]} (note: bytes_written is in the model but NOT in to_dict/json). Error/usage lines go to stderr prefixed 'rac: '. SkillFileExists multi: 'rac: 4 skill files already exist; rac skill install never overwrites:' followed by '  - <path>' lines; single: 'rac: <path> already exists; rac skill install never overwrites'.
parity_cases: rac skill list  (live corpus cwd) -> exit 0, human table, 5 rows in registry order
rac skill list --json -> exit 0, json with schema_version 1 and 5 skills
rac skill list foo -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: skill list takes no skill name'

rac skill install rac-review --dir <tmp/proj> (fresh) -> exit 0, writes <proj>/.claude/skills/rac-review/SKILL.md byte-identical to packaged resource (3700 bytes, sha256 056fac8e...), human 'Installed rac-review skill: <abspath>' + blurb
rac skill install rac-review --dir <proj> again (file exists) -> exit 1, stderr 'rac: <abspath> already exists; rac skill install never overwrites', file untouched
rac skill install --dir <tmp/p2> --json (fresh) -> exit 0, all 5 skills written, json installed:true with 5 abspaths
rac skill install --dir <p4 where 4 of 5 already present> -> exit 1, all-or-nothing refusal listing the 4 existing paths, rac-review NOT written
rac skill install bogus --dir <existing dir> -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: unknown skill: bogus (available: ...)'
rac skill install --dir <nonexistent> -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: not a directory: <dir>'
skill.landmines: 1) Installed file MUST be byte-identical to the packaged resource (loaded via importlib.resources, read_bytes). Rust must embed the same 5 SKILL.md files verbatim (sizes: rac-artifacts 3428, rac-capture 8021, rac-import 6546, rac-ingest 4046, rac-review 3700; sha256 recorded). 2) Human list column width is computed dynamically as max(len(name)) — with the current set that is 13 (rac-artifacts). 3) install all-or-nothing: every destination is checked BEFORE any write; a single collision aborts with NOTHING written. Existing-paths list preserves registry order, filtered to those that exist. 4) json omits bytes_written even though the dataclass has it. 5) dir-check precedes name-validation (ordering matters for which error fires). 6) Paths in output are whatever the caller passed for --dir joined with .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md; the probe showed absolute paths because --dir was absolute — RAC does not abspath-normalize, str(Path(dir)/...) is emitted, so a relative --dir yields relative paths.
skill.env_fs_git: No env vars read. FS: reads packaged resources (asdecided.skills.<name>.SKILL.md); writes <dir>/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (mkdir -p parents, write_bytes, no chmod). No git. No network, no AI.
skill.nondeterminism: None — fully deterministic. No timestamps, no minted ids, registry order is fixed.
skill.reuse: output.rs (json.dumps indent=2 equivalent via pyjson.rs; _bold wrapper for human header), pycompat.rs for path str() semantics. Needs a new embedded-resource module for the SKILL.md bytes (include_bytes!/include_str!). No walk/relationships/gitinfo needed.
skill.harness_gaps: Needs a written-file byte-comparison seam (harness currently compares stdout/stderr/exit; skill install's real contract is the on-disk file bytes + that pre-existing files are untouched). Needs home-dir/tmp isolation to run install into a scratch dir. No id seam, no stdin, no git fixture needed.

hook.argv_shape: rac hook <action> [--style {post-commit,pre-commit}] [--dir DIR] [--json] [--version]. action required positional choices {install,list}; --style default 'post-commit' (DEFAULT_STYLE = first bundled hook), choices enforced by argparse from available_hooks(); --dir default ".". Bundled hooks registry order: post-commit, pre-commit. `list` ignores --style/--dir. `install` writes one hook.
hook.exit_codes: 0 success. 2 usage: invalid --style -> ARGPARSE error (not RACError): stderr two lines 'usage: rac hook [-h] [--version] [--json] [--style {post-commit,pre-commit}]\n                [--dir DIR]\n                {install,list}' then 'rac hook: error: argument --style: invalid choice: 'bogus' (choose from 'post-commit', 'pre-commit')', exit 2 — so HookNotFound is effectively unreachable via CLI. Other exit 2 via _usage_error ('rac: '): --dir not a directory -> 'rac: not a directory: <dir>'; no .git dir -> 'rac: no .git directory in <dir>; run `rac hook install` from a git repository root' (NotAGitWorkTree). 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED): hook file exists -> 'rac: <path> already exists; rac hook install never overwrites'; packaged resource missing (HookResourceMissing).
hook.stdout_kinds: list human (stdout): bold 'Bundled git hooks:' blank then '- <style padded to max width>  <description>' e.g. '- post-commit  Advisory write-cadence nudge after each commit (never blocks).' and '- pre-commit   Validate staged Markdown artifacts before each commit (blocks on errors).' (style col width = max len = len('post-commit')=11). list --json: {"schema_version":"1","hooks":[{"style":..,"description":..},...]}. install human (stdout): 'Installed <style> git hook: <abspath>\n\nGit runs it automatically on each commit. Remove the file to stop it.'. install --json: {"schema_version":"1","installed":true,"hook":{"style":..,"path":<abspath>}}. Errors to stderr.
parity_cases: rac hook list -> exit 0, human 2-row table
rac hook list --json -> exit 0, json 2 hooks

rac hook install --dir <tmp/gitrepo> (git init'd) -> exit 0, writes .git/hooks/post-commit byte-identical to packaged post-commit.sh (572 bytes, sha256 e9b63c87...), mode set +x (rwxr-xr-x), human 'Installed post-commit git hook: <abspath>' + blurb
rac hook install --dir <gitrepo> again -> exit 1, stderr '<path> already exists; ...'
rac hook install --style pre-commit --dir <gitrepo> --json -> exit 0, writes .git/hooks/pre-commit (625 bytes, sha256 f695b750...), json hook.style=pre-commit
rac hook install --dir <non-git dir> -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: no .git directory in <dir>; run `rac hook install` from a git repository root'
rac hook install --style bogus --dir <gitrepo> -> exit 2, argparse usage+error to stderr (invalid choice)
rac hook install --dir <nonexistent> -> exit 2, stderr 'rac: not a directory: <dir>'
hook.landmines: 1) Installed hook MUST be byte-identical to packaged src/asdecided/hooks/<style>.sh (post-commit.sh 572B, pre-commit.sh 625B; note packaged filename has .sh suffix but the INSTALLED file is named <style> with NO extension: .git/hooks/post-commit). 2) The installed file MUST be made executable: chmod adds S_IXUSR|S_IXGRP|S_IXOTH to existing mode -> resulting mode 0755 (rwxr-xr-x) observed. This is a real part of the contract (git won't run non-exec hooks). 3) invalid --style is caught by argparse choices BEFORE the handler, producing an argparse-style usage error, not the RACError HookNotFound message — so the byte-exact stderr is the argparse 'invalid choice' form (2 usage lines wrapped at COLUMNS then the error line). 4) .git must be a directory (is_dir) — a .git FILE (worktree/submodule pointer) fails the check. 5) style col width dynamic = 11. 6) install to <dir>/.git/hooks/<style>, mkdir -p hooks dir if absent.
hook.env_fs_git: No env vars. FS: reads packaged asdecided.hooks.<style>.sh; checks <dir>/.git is_dir; writes <dir>/.git/hooks/<style>, chmod +x. NO git subprocess (only a .git directory existence check via is_dir). No network/AI.
hook.nondeterminism: None — deterministic. (chmod result depends on umask-independent OR of exec bits onto the freshly-written file's mode; Rust should set 0755 or OR the exec bits to match.)
hook.reuse: output.rs/_bold, pyjson.rs for json. Embedded-resource module for the 2 hook scripts (shared with skill's resource approach). No gitinfo needed (only a filesystem is_dir(.git) check, not a git call). Effort S — nearly identical shape to skill, minus all-or-nothing.
hook.harness_gaps: Same written-file byte-comparison + file-mode (executable bit) comparison seam as skill; plus a git-fixture seam that only needs `git init` (a bare .git directory suffices — no commits needed, since the code only stats .git). No id/stdin/rev seams needed.

watchkeeper.argv_shape: rac watchkeeper [directory] [--base REV] [--head REV] [--format {human,json,github}] [--json] [--fail-on {error,warning,none}] [--no-annotate] [--version]. directory optional positional default None -> resolves to 'rac' if ./rac is a dir else '.'. --base default 'main'. --head default None (=working tree at directory). --format default 'human'. --json is an alias that OVERRIDES --format to json (output_format = 'json' if args.json else args.format). --fail-on default 'error'. --no-annotate sets dest=annotate store_false (default True); only affects github format's stderr annotations. base/head each may name an existing directory (used as-is) OR a git revision materialized via `git archive`.
watchkeeper.exit_codes: 0: success and no failure per policy. 2 usage via _usage_error ('rac: '): directory not a dir -> 'rac: not a directory: <dir>'; NotAGitRepository -> 'rac: not a git repository: <dir>' or 'rac: git executable not found'; RevisionNotFound -> 'rac: unknown revision: <rev>'. 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED) driven by failure policy: if --fail-on none -> always 0; else if report.review_recommended (recommendations non-empty) -> 1; else if --fail-on warning AND report.has_warnings (any finding severity==warning) -> 1; else 0. NOTE the review-recommended set is narrow: only validation_regression (newly_invalid), broken_relationship (new relationship issues), and the 5 RECOMMENDING_FINDINGS intent codes (specificity_regression, constraint_weakened, constraint_removed, acceptance_criteria_removed, success_measures_removed). unlinked_scope/ambiguity_introduced/relationship_impact are warnings/info that do NOT recommend review (so they only fail under --fail-on warning).
watchkeeper.stdout_kinds: human (stdout, render_watchkeeper_human): starts bold 'RAC Watchkeeper' then '===============' then blank, 'Directory:  <dir>', 'Comparing:  <base> → <head>' (unicode arrow U+2192, two spaces after label), sections 'Changed Artifacts'/'Validation'/'Relationships'/'Repository Changes'/optional 'Findings (N)'/'Review'. Change lines '  + <path>  (<type>)' icons +/~/- for added/modified/removed; '  No product artifact changes detected.' when none. Deltas '  Valid:    <b> → <h>'. Newly-invalid lines use red '✗', newly-valid green '✓', new issues yellow '!', findings yellow '!' (warning) or '·' (info). Review verdict: recommended -> yellow 'Review recommended.' + '  Reasons:' + '    · <reason>  [<code>]'; else green '✓ Nothing requiring attention.'. --json (stdout): json.dumps(report.to_dict(), indent=2) with schema_version 1; keys directory,base,head,changes[],validation{base,head,newly_invalid,newly_valid},relationships{base,head,new_issues,resolved_issues},stats{total,by_type},findings[],review{recommended,reasons[]}; coverage is a float (0.0). --format github (stdout): GitHub-flavored Markdown step-summary starting '# RAC Watchkeeper' with tables; ALSO (unless --no-annotate) workflow-command annotation lines to STDERR e.g. '::warning file=rac/d2.md::unlinked_scope: New artifact declares no relationships and nothing references it.' (::error for validation_regression/broken_relationship/recommending findings, ::notice for info). ANSI color only when stdout is a tty (output.rs color gating).
parity_cases: git fixture: base commit has rac/d1.md (valid decision); working tree adds rac/d2.md. `rac watchkeeper rac/` -> exit 0, human report shows '+ d2.md  (decision)', Valid 1→2, a warning finding [unlinked_scope] on d2.md, verdict 'Nothing requiring attention.'

same fixture `rac watchkeeper rac/ --json` -> exit 0, json.to_dict with changes[0].change=added, id/title from the artifact, base_status null head_status valid, relationships.base.orphaned=1 coverage 0.0, review.recommended false
same fixture `--format github` -> exit 0, Markdown table on stdout AND '::warning file=rac/d2.md::unlinked_scope: ...' on stderr; with `--no-annotate` stderr is empty (0 bytes)
same fixture `--fail-on warning` -> exit 1 (warning finding present, though review not recommended)
same fixture `--fail-on none` -> exit 0 always
`rac watchkeeper rac/ --base <nonexistent-rev>` in a git repo -> exit 2 'rac: unknown revision: <rev>'
`rac watchkeeper rac/nope` -> exit 2 'rac: not a directory: rac/nope'
non-git dir with a rac/ subdir, default base=main -> exit 2 'rac: not a git repository: rac/' (base 'main' is not an existing dir so git resolution is attempted and fails)
base as an existing DIRECTORY path (not a rev): `rac watchkeeper head_dir --base base_dir` -> no git at all, directory-vs-directory comparison, exit 0

validation-regression fixture (head artifact still classifies as its type but fails validation) -> newly_invalid non-empty -> review recommended -> exit 1, verdict 'Review recommended.' reason validation_regression
watchkeeper.landmines: 1) BIG dependency chain, none of it ported: watchkeeper -> services.compare(load_state, compare_states) -> services.diff (Diff model + requirement-level diffing) which is a Rust STUB ('diff' subcommand: 'not yet implemented'); -> services.intent (~310 LOC of deterministic text heuristics: ambiguity terms, mandatory/hedge terms 'must/shall' vs 'should/may/could', acceptance/success section detection, specificity regression) with a fixed sort key (severity!=warning, code, path, detail); -> services.revisions (git archive materialization). Porting watchkeeper REQUIRES porting diff + compare + intent + revisions first. 2) git subprocess contract (revisions.py, THE only git-writing path but it never mutates .git): (a) `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` cwd=directory (repository_root); (b) `git rev-parse --verify --quiet <rev>^{commit}` cwd=repo_root (RevisionNotFound if rc!=0); (c) `git archive --format=tar <rev> -- <pathspec>` cwd=repo_root, pathspec = corpus subpath relative to repo root (or '.' if empty/'.'); tar is extracted to a tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix='rac-watchkeeper-') with tarfile filter='data', then corpus = tmp/subpath (mkdir -p if archive missing subpath -> empty 'everything added' corpus). A nonzero archive rc is NOT an error — it means the subpath doesn't exist at rev -> empty corpus. FileNotFoundError on git binary -> NotAGitRepository('git executable not found'). 3) subpath computed via os.path.relpath(os.path.abspath(directory), root) — path semantics must match. 4) --json overrides --format (json wins even with --format github). 5) The unicode arrow ' → ' and check/cross glyphs are literal in output. 6) coverage renders as a float (0.0) in json. 7) github annotations go to STDERR, step-summary to STDOUT, in the SAME invocation. 8) default directory resolution: None -> 'rac' if Path('rac').is_dir() else '.'. 9) load_state uses collect_corpus + repository_from_corpus + relationships + validate — heavy but those pieces largely exist in Rust (classify/validate/relationships/walk).
watchkeeper.env_fs_git: Env: none read directly by watchkeeper (DECIDED_NO_CACHE etc. affect shared corpus loading). FS: reads corpus dirs; creates+removes a TemporaryDirectory (prefix rac-watchkeeper-) per materialized revision; extracts tar into it; no writes to the repo. Git: 3 subprocess forms above (rev-parse show-toplevel, rev-parse --verify --quiet, archive --format=tar), all read-only, cwd-sensitive, capture_output. No network/AI.
watchkeeper.nondeterminism: TempDir path is random but never appears in output (only the corpus-relative and label paths do) — no seam needed for output parity. Git must be present and the fixture committed; base/head labels in output are the argv strings, not resolved shas. Fully deterministic OUTPUT given a fixed git fixture. One seam concern: findings sort is total-ordered by (severity,code,path,detail) so stable. No id minting, no timestamps in output.
watchkeeper.reuse: gitinfo.rs already has run_git + `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (repository_root) — extend it with rev-parse --verify and git archive + tar extraction. classify.rs, validate.rs, relationships.rs, walk.rs cover load_state internals. output.rs (_bold/_red/_green/_yellow, tty color gating), pyjson.rs (indent=2, float formatting for coverage). NEW modules required: diff (requirement model diff), compare (load_state/compare_states), intent (~310 LOC heuristics), revisions (archive+tar), watchkeeper (report assembly), and 3 renderers (human/json/github incl. stderr annotations). This is the largest of the three by far.
watchkeeper.blockers: services.diff is unported (Rust stub) and is a hard prerequisite — the requirement-level Diff model feeds intent analysis. compare and intent services do not exist in Rust. A tar-extraction dependency (or a `git archive | tar -x` shell) is needed; the Rust engine currently has no tar reader. These must be ported/added before watchkeeper can reach parity.
watchkeeper.harness_gaps: Needs a GIT FIXTURE seam with real commits (base commit + working-tree deltas) — richer than hook's bare `git init`. Needs to compare stdout AND stderr separately (github mode splits step-summary vs annotations across the two streams). Needs deterministic corpus fixtures for the intent heuristics (constraint weakening etc.) to exercise the review-recommended exit-1 path. No id/stdin seam.

decisions-for.argv_shape: rac decisions-for <path> [directory=.] [--top-level] [--recursive] [--json] [--version] [-h]. `path` is required positional (repo file/dir, POSIX/native, repo-relative or absolute); `directory` optional positional (corpus to scan, default '.'). `--top-level` sets recursive=False; `--recursive` is a no-op affirmation of the default. Flags come from version_parent + json_parent; --top-level/--recursive declared inline (NOT scope_parent).
decisions-for.exit_codes: 0 for EVERY valid query — governed path, ungoverned in-repo path, and outside-repository/escaping path all exit 0 (a query always succeeds, REQ-004). 2 (EXIT_USAGE) for: `directory` not a directory, missing required `path`, extra positionals. No path exists on disk is fine (pure string matching).
decisions-for.reuse: retrieve.rs ALREADY ports the whole scope engine for the MCP find_decisions path mode: `find_decisions_path_payload(directory, path)` emits the byte-identical `{schema_version,query,in_repository,decisions}` payload (= ScopeLookupResult.to_dict, so --json is essentially free); plus `governing_decisions`, `scope_rows_from_items`, `normalize_query`, `entry_covers`, `glob_match_at`, `classify_scope_entry`, `normalized_scope_path`, `repository_root`, `py_casefold`, `is_live_decision`, `artifact_status`, `artifact_identifier`, `extract_relationships_full`. output.rs already renders the `↳` glyph (find). Only new work: the human renderer (render_decisions_for_human) + CLI arg parsing + threading recursive.
parity_cases: argv `decisions-for rac/prompts rac/` in /home/user/rac-core → exit 0, single-row human output governed by RAC-KTQ63DR3G4YG (ADR-018, applies-to rac/)
argv `decisions-for src/asdecided/mcp/server.py rac/` → exit 0, TWO rows (ADR-023 src/asdecided/, ADR-033 src/asdecided/mcp/) proving casefold+path sort and multi-row alignment
argv `decisions-for rac/prompts rac/ --json` → exit 0, byte-compare full ScopeLookupResult JSON
argv `decisions-for /etc/passwd rac/` and `--json` → exit 0, `in_repository:false`, query is raw stripped input, empty decisions
argv `decisions-for zzz/nowhere.txt rac/` → exit 0, `No decisions declare scope over 'zzz/nowhere.txt'.`
argv `decisions-for rac/prompts rac/ --top-level` → exit 0, empty (recursion off skips rac/decisions/) — pins the recursive threading
argv `decisions-for src/asdecided/cli.py /nonexistent_dir` → exit 2, stderr `rac: not a directory: /nonexistent_dir`
argv `decisions-for` (no path) → exit 2, argparse required-arg error
decisions-for.landmines: (1) rust `find_decisions_path_payload` HARDCODES recursive: it calls `corpus_items(directory, true)`; the CLI must thread recursive=not top_level, so the port cannot just call that helper for --top-level. (2) Human status/title empty-fallback is the em-dash `—` (d.status or '—', d.title or '—'). (3) sort key is (id.casefold(), path) via py_casefold — already available. (4) outside-repo query value in both human and JSON is the RAW stripped input path (not normalized). (5) column widths: id_w=max id len, status_w=max(status or '—' len); continuation indent is `' '*id_w + '  ' + ' '*status_w + '  '`.
decisions-for.env_fs_git: Reads corpus dir (walk_corpus) and locates repo root via repository_root (nearest ancestor .decided/, ADR-018). No writes, no git, no env vars beyond DECIDED_NO_CACHE (cache is bypassed anyway on this path).
decisions-for.nondeterminism: none — pure function of corpus bytes + query path
decisions-for.harness_gaps: Needs a fixture whose corpus sits under a repo root containing .decided/ so repository_root resolves — the live rac/ corpus works (repo root is /home/user/rac-core). Otherwise reuses existing walk/json comparison. No new harness feature required.

eval.argv_shape: rac eval [--check | --update-baseline] [--json] [--root ROOT] [--queries QUERIES] [--baseline BASELINE] [--config CONFIG] [--version] [-h]. NO positionals. --check/--update-baseline are a mutually-exclusive group. Defaults: root=tests/eval/corpus, queries=tests/eval/queries.json, baseline=tests/eval/baseline.json, config=tests/eval/eval-config.json (resolved against CWD).
eval.exit_codes: 0: clean report (default), gate PASS (--check), baseline written (--update-baseline). 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED): gate failure under --check (one or more fired rules). 2 (EXIT_USAGE): any EvalUsageError (corpus not a dir, baseline/queries/config not found, malformed query set/baseline/config), the --check/--update-baseline mutex conflict, and unrecognized extra positionals.
eval.stdout_kinds:
Default report (human) to stdout: `Overall` then header ` P@1 R@1 P@3 R@3 P@5 R@5` then row ` 1.000 0.903 0.417 1.000 0.250 1.000` (format `{v:>8.3f}` per cell, 2-space lead), then ` negative_violations: 0`, blank, `By category` + grouped rows ` {p:>6.3f} {r:>6.3f}` under header ` P@1 R@5`, blank, `By tool` block, blank, `Violations` then ` none` or offender lines. --json: full scorecard `{metrics, metadata, per_query}` indent=2 ensure_ascii=False, floats as Python repr (e.g. `0.416667`, `0.25`, `1.0`). --check pass: stdout `rac eval: gate PASS`. --check fail: one line per rule to stdout, e.g. `FAIL [negative_violations] overall.negative_violations: limit -1, current 0`, `FAIL [floor] overall.p_at_1: floor 1.500000, current 1.000000`, `FAIL [regression] overall.p_at_1: baseline 1.500000, current 1.000000`. --update-baseline: stdout `rac eval: baseline updated -> ` and writes render_metrics_json + '\n'. EvalUsageError to stderr as `rac eval: ` e.g. `rac eval: baseline not found: /nope/baseline.json`, `rac eval: corpus not found or not a directory: /nonexistent_corpus`, `rac eval: malformed query set:

: expected a non-empty 'cases' list`.
eval.reuse: resolve.rs `search_index` (search_artifacts cases, verbatim (match_rank,path) order); relationships.rs `incoming_references` + `relationships_from_corpus` + `resolve_in_index` + `index_from_corpus` (get_related cases); build_repository_index; walk.rs; pyjson for float-exact serialization; output.rs version seam. Needs a NEW eval.rs: QueryCase/CaseResult/Scorecard, JSON loaders with the exact error strings, score_case (P@k/R@k over k∈{1,3,5}), _overall/_grouped aggregation, corpus_hash/query_set_hash (sha256), evaluate_gate, and 3 renderers.
parity_cases: argv `eval` in /home/user/rac-core (uses tests/eval fixtures) → exit 0, human tables byte-compare (all metrics 1.0 except overall p_at_3=0.417/p_at_5=0.250/r_at_1=0.903)
argv `eval --json` → exit 0, byte-compare metrics + per_query + the two DETERMINISTIC hashes; MASK metadata.generated_at and metadata.lore_version
argv `eval --check` → exit 0, stdout `rac eval: gate PASS`

forced-fail fixture (config floors.overall.p_at_1=1.5, floors.negative_violations=-1, baseline.overall.p_at_1=1.5): argv `eval --check --root <sb>/corpus --queries ... --baseline base-fail.json --config cfg-fail.json` → exit 1, three FAIL lines in order negative_violations, floor, regression
argv `eval --check --baseline /nope/baseline.json` → exit 2, stderr `rac eval: baseline not found: /nope/baseline.json`
argv `eval --root /nonexistent_corpus` → exit 2, stderr `rac eval: corpus not found or not a directory: /nonexistent_corpus`
argv `eval --check --update-baseline` → exit 2, argparse mutex `rac eval: error: argument --update-baseline: not allowed with argument --check`

argv `eval --update-baseline --baseline /tmp/out.json --root <sb>/corpus --queries <sb>/queries.json` → exit 0, stdout `rac eval: baseline updated -> /tmp/out.json`, written file = render_metrics_json + single trailing '\n' (ends `}\n`)
malformed inputs: `{"cases":"x"}` queries → exit 2 `...expected a non-empty 'cases' list`; `{}` baseline → `...expected a metrics object`; `{}` config → `...expected 'floors' and 'tolerance'`
eval.landmines: (1) --json metadata carries TWO nondeterministic fields: `generated_at` (wall clock, datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()) and `lore_version` (__version__ from git-describe) — harness MUST mask both. corpus_hash and query_set_hash ARE deterministic and must be compared. (2) The gate enforces ONLY p_at_1 and r_at_5 (see _gated_pairs) — a floor on p_at_5 or p_at_3 is SILENTLY IGNORED (I confirmed a p_at_5=0.9 floor did not fire). (3) GateFailure.render formatting: negative rule uses `limit {t:.0f}, current {c:.0f}`; floor/regression use `{label} {t:.6f}, current {c:.6f}` where label is 'floor' for RULE_FLOOR else 'baseline'. (4) A gated metric absent from current run → RULE_FLOOR with current 0.0, threshold=floor or 0.0. (5) float serialization must match Python json.dumps repr (0.25 not 0.250000, 1.0 not 1); use pyjson. (6) metrics rounded to 6 decimals via round(); Python banker's rounding. (7) --json is IGNORED under --check and --update-baseline (mode wins). (8) corpus_hash walks find_markdown_files (recursive=True, sorted, dotted-dir-skipped) prefixing each rel POSIX path + b'\0' + bytes + b'\0'; query_set_hash is sha256 of raw file bytes.
eval.blockers: none blocking, but eval's correctness is DOWNSTREAM of search_index and incoming_references being byte-exact — a get_related case that fails to resolve raises EvalUsageError `get_related case <id>: query <q> did not resolve to an artifact in <root>` (exit 2). If those ported surfaces drift, eval scores drift.
eval.env_fs_git: Reads root corpus + queries/baseline/config JSON. --update-baseline WRITES the baseline file (probe only in /tmp). lore_version pulls the version string (git-describe-derived, same seam as `rac --version`). No other git.
eval.nondeterminism: metadata.generated_at (clock) and metadata.lore_version (version) — both diagnostic, excluded from the gate; harness masks them in --json. Scored path (metrics/per_query/hashes) fully deterministic.

Needs a written-file comparison for --update-baseline (write to /tmp, compare bytes incl. trailing newline). Forced-gate-failure fixtures (tampered baseline/config) must be added under fixtures. Everything else reuses existing corpus fixtures at tests/eval/.

migrate.argv_shape: rac migrate <target> <directory> [--dry-run] [--top-level] [--recursive] [--json] [--version] [-h]. `target` positional with choices={metadata} (required); `directory` positional required. --top-level → recursive=False; --recursive no-op default. Flags from version_parent + json_parent + scope_parent (--top-level/--recursive).
migrate.exit_codes: 0 (EXIT_OK): completed migration OR dry-run, INCLUDING 'nothing to migrate' (a valid outcome). 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED): MalformedRepositoryConfig or IdGenerationExhausted → stderr `rac: <msg>`. 2 (EXIT_USAGE): missing repository config → `rac: no repository identity found at or above <dir>; run \`rac init\` to establish a repository key first`; directory not a directory → `rac: not a directory: <dir>`; bad target choice; missing directory arg.
migrate.stdout_kinds: Human to stdout. Dry-run leads with bold `Dry run — no files were written.` + blank. Migrated block: `Would migrate`/`Migrated <n> artifact(s):` then rows `  <path:width>  <id>  (<type>)` e.g. `  /tmp/mig-sb/adr-001-legacy.md  RAC-KXBTPB2G167E  (decision)`; if none: `<verb> 0 artifact(s) — nothing to migrate.`. Skipped block (ONLY when unknowns exist): blank + bold `Skipped <n> unrecognized document(s):` + `  - <path>` lines. Trailing: blank + `<total> file(s): <m> migrated, <a> already canonical, <s> skipped (unknown type).`. --json to stdout indent=2: `{schema_version:'1', directory, recursive, dry_run, summary:{total_files,migrated,already_canonical,skipped_unknown}, files:[{path,status,id,type}]}` where status ∈ migrated|already-canonical|skipped-unknown and id/type are null unless migrated. Errors to stderr.
migrate.reuse:
walk.rs walk_corpus (sorted order); build_repository_index (seed issued-id set from repository_root); classify/spec_for (spec_for(type) is None → skipped-unknown); frontmatter parse to detect existing metadata/metadata_issues → already-canonical; load_repository_config (init service). Needs a NEW idgen port (generate_id: Crockford base32, 8-char ms-timestamp + 4-char CSPRNG) and render_frontmatter (`---
schema_version: 1
id: <id>
type: <type>
---
`).
parity_cases: COPY tests/fixtures/migrate (adr-001-legacy.md, canonical.md, notes.md, .decided/) to /tmp. argv `migrate metadata <sb> --dry-run --json` → exit 0; compare ALL fields except files[].id which is a minted ID (mask it). Confirms adr-001 classifies decision, canonical.md already-canonical, notes.md skipped-unknown, walk order alphabetical
argv `migrate metadata <sb> --dry-run` → exit 0, human output byte-compare with the minted id masked (`RAC-<12>`)

argv `migrate metadata <sb>` (real write) then re-inspect adr-001-legacy.md: envelope prepended, body bytes preserved (`---
schema_version: 1
id: RAC-...
type: decision
---
# A Legacy Decision...`) — needs written-file comparison with id-masking

second run on the already-written sandbox → exit 0, `Migrated 0 artifact(s) — nothing to migrate.` + Skipped notes.md + `3 file(s): 0 migrated, 2 already canonical, 1 skipped (unknown type).` — FULLY DETERMINISTIC (no new IDs), best pure-byte parity case
argv `migrate metadata <sb> --top-level --dry-run` on a nested corpus → exit 0, only top-level file considered (nested subdir skipped)
argv `migrate metadata <dir-without-.decided>` → exit 2, stderr `rac: no repository identity found at or above <dir>; run \`rac init\` to establish a repository key first`
argv `migrate metadata /nonexistent_dir_xyz` → exit 2, stderr `rac: not a directory: /nonexistent_dir_xyz`
argv `migrate frobnicate <sb>` → exit 2, argparse `rac migrate: error: argument target: invalid choice: 'frobnicate' (choose from 'metadata')`
argv `migrate metadata` (no dir) → exit 2, argparse required-arg error
migrate.landmines: (1) MINTED IDs are the core problem: generate_id = wall-clock-ms Crockford segment + CSPRNG segment; the id appears in HUMAN output, --json (files[].id), AND the written file bytes for every `migrated` file. No CLI-level id seam exists (the Python _DEFAULT_ID_GENERATOR is module-level, injectable only in-process). So byte-parity for any migrated file REQUIRES either an id-masking normalizer in the harness or an id seam in BOTH binaries (the oracle CLI has none — cannot be added to Python). Best deterministic cases are the 'nothing to migrate' states (already-canonical + skipped-unknown only). (2) migration NEVER touches a file with ANY frontmatter — valid, malformed, OR unterminated (product.metadata is not None OR product.metadata_issues) → already-canonical; validation owns broken envelopes. (3) MalformedRepositoryConfig (exit 1) message embeds PyYAML's multi-line parser diagnostic (`invalid YAML: while parsing a flow sequence ...`) — NOT byte-reproducible in Rust; treat like argparse usage bodies (out of strict parity scope). (4) issued-id set is seeded (uppercased) from build_repository_index over the repo ROOT (Path(config_path).parent.parent), and IDs dedupe within-run + against index — bounded 5 attempts → IdGenerationExhausted (exit 1). (5) --top-level threads recursive=not top_level. (6) Skipped block omitted entirely when no unknowns. (7) Written envelope prepend is byte-exact: envelope.encode('utf-8') + original_bytes, body untouched.
migrate.blockers: id seam / id-masking is required infra for parity on migrated files — flag to harness. Not a code blocker (idgen is portable), but a parity-comparison blocker.
migrate.env_fs_git: Reads corpus (walk_corpus) + repo config (.decided/config.yaml via load_repository_config) + repo index. WRITES files (prepends frontmatter) unless --dry-run. Uses wall clock + CSPRNG for id minting. No git.
migrate.nondeterminism: Minted artifact IDs (time+CSPRNG) in every migrated row/file. Needs an id seam OR harness id-masking. 'nothing to migrate' runs are fully deterministic.
migrate.harness_gaps: (1) written-file comparison — migrate mutates the corpus; harness must diff post-write file bytes (with id masking). (2) id-masking normalizer (regex RAC-[0-9A-Z]{12} → placeholder) OR an injected deterministic id generator exposed to both binaries. (3) a fixture directory WITHOUT .decided/ for the missing-config path. (4) a sandbox-copy step (never mutate tests/fixtures/migrate in place).

export --agent-rules.argv_shape: rac export [directory=.] --agent-rules [--check] [--client {claude|agents|cursor|copilot} ...repeatable] [--json] [--out ROOT]. Handler: cmd_export -> _cmd_agent_rules in src/asdecided/cli.py:796,878; service src/asdecided/services/agent_rules.py; renderers render_agent_rules_human (output/human.py:1327), render_agent_rules_json (output/json.py:382). --agent-rules is one of the mutually-exclusive export-mode group; --check and --client are only meaningful with it (guarded at cli.py:812-815). Output root resolution _agent_rules_root (cli.py:~780): if --out given use it; else path=directory.rstrip('/'); if path.name=='rac' -> path.parent (or '.' when parent is '' or '.'); else the directory itself. So `export rac/ --agent-rules` writes into '.' (root prints as '.').
export --agent-rules.exit_codes: generate ok -> 0; check clean -> 0; check drift (any file stale OR missing block) -> 1 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED, cli.py:912-913); `--check` without `--agent-rules` -> 2 (_usage_error '--check requires --agent-rules'); `--client` without `--agent-rules` -> 2 ('--client requires --agent-rules'); invalid --client value -> 2 (argparse choices, message 'rac export: error: argument --client: invalid choice: ...' — already enforced by the shared rust arg parser, verified identical); OSError while writing -> 2 (_usage_error 'cannot write under {root}: {exc}'); directory not a dir -> 2 ('not a directory: {dir}'). All measured, not documented.
export --agent-rules.stdout_kinds: Human (default, stdout): first line _bold title 'Agent Rules' (generate) or 'Agent Rules — drift check' (check), then a '=' underline of matching length, blank, 'Corpus digest: <sha256hex>', 'Output root:   <root>', blank, then per-file '  <icon> <path>  [<state>]' where icon is '+' (written), '~' (updated), green '✓' (in-sync), red '✗' (stale/missing) — color gated on sys.stdout.isatty() so NON-TTY emits no ANSI (verified via cat -A: plain '✓'/'✗' bytes). Verdict line: generate -> green '✓ Wrote/updated N file(s).' or green '✓ All targets already in sync — nothing to write.'; check clean -> green '✓ In sync — every present target matches the corpus.'; check drift -> red '✗ Drift — N file(s) stale or missing the block.' then '  Regenerate: rac export --agent-rules'. Files listed in fixed TARGETS order: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, .cursor/rules (subset when --client given). --json (stdout): json.dumps(result.to_dict(), indent=2) => {"mode":"check|generate","digest":"<hex>","root":"<root>","files":[{"client":..,"path":..,"state":..}]}. Managed block written INTO each file (bytes matter more than stdout): line1 '<!-- BEGIN RAC MANAGED BLOCK (digest: <hex>) -->', line2 '<!-- Managed by `rac export --agent-rules`. Edit decisions in rac/, not here; content outside this block is preserved. -->', '## Settled decisions (RAC)', '', 'These decisions are already accepted. Do not re-open or contradict them; ask the `lore` MCP tools (`get_artifact`, `search_artifacts`) for the full text before proposing a change that touches one.', '', then per live decision '- **<identifier>** — <title>[ _(<category>)_]' (category suffix only when present), or '_No live decisions recorded yet._' when empty, then '<!-- END RAC MANAGED BLOCK -->'.
parity_cases: generate fresh: fixture=/tmp sandbox proj/ with proj/rac/decisions/adr-001-thing.md (Status: Accepted, Category: Architecture, id RAC-AAAA1111AAAA); argv `export <sb>/proj/rac --agent-rules`; expect exit 0, stdout root '<sb>/proj', four files [written], AND compare the four written files byte-for-byte (CLAUDE.md digest aa720a71588b5c60385eccf68764a2330d4d951aa4f59fbb5f7291a398cda7ae, entry line '- **RAC-AAAA1111AAAA** — ADR-001: A Thing _(Architecture)_').

check drift stale: corrupt CLAUDE.md BEGIN digest to deadbeef; argv `--agent-rules --check`; exit 1, CLAUDE.md [stale], drift verdict '✗ Drift — 1 file(s) stale or missing the block.'

export --agent-rules.landmines:
(1) Digest byte-parity is the crux: sha256 over json.dumps([{category,identifier,title}], sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False). sort_keys reorders entry keys to category,identifier,title; ensure_ascii=False means non-ASCII titles stay raw UTF-8 (Rust must NOT \u-escape). null (not omitted) for missing category. Any deviation changes the digest, which is embedded in the BEGIN marker and cascades into --check drift. (2) Liveness predicate is spec-driven: decision is live when Status first-non-empty-line casefold=='accepted' AND status not in spec_for('decision').retired_status (casefolded) — do not hard-code Superseded/Deprecated; read spec.retired_status. (3) Entry order sort key is (identifier.casefold(), identifier) — casefold primary, exact tiebreak. (4) merge_managed_block splice rules (agent_rules.py:299): existing None/blank -> block+'
'; existing WITH block -> replace [BEGIN..END+len(END)] keeping before/after verbatim, ensure single trailing '
'; existing WITHOUT block -> existing.rstrip('
') + '

' + block + '
'. (5) root resolution '.' vs directory (name=='rac' -> parent, '' /'.' parent -> '.'). (6) generate write-skip when embedded_digest(existing)==projection.digest -> state in-sync, file untouched (idempotence/mtime). (7) Colour gated on isatty; icons '+'/'~'/'✓'/'✗'. (8) category/title come from _category (first non-empty line of ## Category) and product.title or identifier fallback.

export --agent-rules.nondeterminism: None — digest is a content hash, no timestamps, no minted ids, deterministic TARGETS order. Fully reproducible. No id seam needed. Harness needs: written-file byte comparison and cwd/home isolation (default root resolves to '.').
export --agent-rules.blockers: none — all backing infra (walk, spec.retired_status, identity, colour/json output) already ported.

export --okf.landmines:
(1) DOMINANT parity risk: created/updated and log.md day grouping derive from git commit timestamps WITH the committer's stored TZ offset (git log --format=%cI; last_committed=`git log -1`, first_committed=`git log --reverse` first line). Values are datetime.isoformat() of the parsed %cI, preserving offset e.g. '+01:00'. Requires a git fixture with pinned committer date+offset; TZ env does NOT affect %cI. (2) rust gitinfo.rs has last_committed + isoformat_roundtrip but NOT first_committed — must add `git log --reverse --format=%cI` first-line reader; created=isoformat_roundtrip(first line), updated=isoformat_roundtrip(last). (3) log.md date key is committed.date().isoformat() = the YYYY-MM-DD prefix in the stored offset (no tz conversion). (4) bundle keys use os.path.relpath(art.path, root) — replicate relpath semantics; dict written in sorted(bundle.items()) order via mkdir -p per parent. (5) body = split_frontmatter(text).body.strip() (leading+trailing whitespace trimmed) — reuse frontmatter.rs. (6) citations: only edges where edge.from==art.id AND edge.to resolves to a corpus id (by_id); rendered '- [<target.title>](<relpath-of-target>)'; unresolved edges omitted. (7) OKF_TYPE map (requirement->Requirement, decision->ADR, design->Design, roadmap->Roadmap, prompt->Prompt) must be added to rust (only OKF_TYPES list exists in validate.rs). (8) each file ends with exactly one '
' ('
'.join(lines)+'
'). (9) OSError message embeds Python errno string e.g. 'cannot write /tmp/x/sub: [Errno 20] Not a directory: '/tmp/x/sub/decisions'' — Rust std::io::Error Display differs, so the error-path bytes will NOT match without shaping to the Python '[Errno N] <strerror>: '<path>'' form (and the path in exc is the internal failing subpath, not --out). (10) uncaught ValueError on index.md/log.md filename collision (exit 1 traceback).
export --okf.reuse: export.rs (build_corpus_export, CorpusExport, ExportArtifact{id,type,title,path,tags}, ExportRelationship{from,to}), gitinfo.rs (last_committed, isoformat_roundtrip — ADD first_committed), frontmatter.rs (split_frontmatter), recency wiring (RecencyReport-equivalent with_creation). No pyjson (Markdown text).
export --okf.env_fs_git: env: none material (TZ ignored by %cI). fs: reads every artifact file (for bodies) + writes the bundle tree under out (mkdir parents). git: YES — `git log -1 --format=%cI` per file (updated) and `git log --reverse --format=%cI` per file (created); degrades gracefully to omitted fields / log placeholder when git absent or file untracked.
export --okf.nondeterminism: created/updated frontmatter fields and log.md date grouping are git-timestamp-derived (committer offset preserved) — needs a git fixture seam with pinned GIT_COMMITTER_DATE + fixed offset. Everything else (ordering, index, bodies) is deterministic.
export --okf.blockers: Rust gitinfo lacks first_committed (small add). No infra the engine fundamentally lacks — recency/git already present. Harness lacks a pinned-git-timestamp fixture builder.
export --okf.harness_gaps: Git fixture with reproducible committer dates+TZ offsets (for created/updated/log parity); recursive written-tree byte comparison (many files, not stdout); cwd isolation for default 'okf-bundle' out; a way to assert the OSError-path byte string (Python '[Errno N]...' shape) — likely accept as a known divergence or shape Rust error text.

export --html.landmines: (1) BLOCKER: byte-parity requires embedding the EXACT packaged shell bytes (lore-portal-shell.html, lore-web @ ed4dd42) into the Rust build (include_str!), and re-vendoring in lockstep whenever the Python package updates the shell — any shell byte drift breaks the whole 3.3MB output. (2) escape order/exactness: '</'->'<\/' then '<!--'->'<!--' (two literal str replaces on the serialized JSON, applied to the whole payload). (3) seam string is exact with NO whitespace inside the element; the count!=1 guard maps to PortalSeamMissing (exit 2) — with a compile-time embed this can't fire, but keep the guard for contract fidelity. (4) render_export_json must already be byte-identical (it is — the default export mode is ported and shared here). (5) writes a single file (not a tree); trailing bytes are whatever the shell ends with (no added newline). (6) OSError message bytes carry the Python '[Errno N] <strerror>: '<path>'' shape (Rust io::Error Display differs) — same error-path caveat as OKF.
export --html.reuse: portal.py logic is thin: reuse render_export_json (already ported/shared), output.rs. Needs a new embedded shell asset + seam-replace/escape helper. PortalShellMissing is unreachable once embedded; PortalSeamMissing guard retained.
export --html.env_fs_git: env: none. fs: reads corpus (via build_corpus_export), reads packaged shell (embedded in rust), writes one HTML file to out. git: NONE.
export --html.nondeterminism: None in the wrapping — shell is static, render_export_json is deterministic. Byte-parity hinges solely on the embedded shell matching the Python package's shell.
export --html.blockers: Vendoring the 182KB lore-portal-shell.html into the rust crate (include_str!) so the emitted HTML is byte-identical; must stay version-locked to rac/templates/portal/. Otherwise the mode is trivial.
export --html.harness_gaps: Written-file byte comparison for a single large HTML file; a mechanism to keep the vendored shell in sync between Python package data and the Rust embed (or a test asserting the two shell blobs are identical); cwd isolation for the default lore-export.html output path.
